I came into this expecting a pretty standard historical mystery, but it ended up being more character-driven than I anticipated. The post-WWI setting is well done and Maisie is an interesting protagonist, part investigator, part psychologist. I thought the mystery itself was a bit slow to get going and the resolution felt a little too neat for my taste, but the atmosphere and period detail really pulled me through. It's not as action-packed as some mysteries I've read, which caught me off guard at first, but once I adjusted my expectations I actually appreciated the slower pace. Definitely picking up the next one in the series. If you're looking for something atmospheric and thoughtful rather than fast-paced, this is worth your time.

It's hard to classify this movie. I guess it's a "drama", but nothing particularly dramatic happens. Mainly it's a chance to watch the interplay of personalities, mainly between the father and the two brothers. Oh, and there's some really pretty scenery and some fancy fishing. To enjoy this movie, you have to be prepared to sit back, relax, and be receptive to a slow unfolding of the story's subleties. A little bit like the way people enjoy fishing, come to think of it. The DVD version is great for maximizing the scenic aspect of the movie - this really requires the wide-screen format. Not too much in the way of bonus materials, though.
It was a present and the recipients like it. Will recommend to other people

My vet suggested trying these joint chews for my lab's back issues, so I picked up the large breed size. At nearly $60 for 120 soft chews, I expected to see some noticeable improvement. After going through almost the entire container over two months, I can't say it made any real difference. She still seems just as stiff getting up in the mornings. The chews smell pretty strong and leave an oily residue on your fingers, which isn't great. She did eat them willingly enough, mixed into her food. Maybe this works better for dogs with mild joint discomfort, but for the price tag, I wanted more results. I'll be looking into other options before spending this much again. Not terrible, but not worth the cost for us.

She's messy, grieving, and totally unprepared, and that's what makes it work. The trail scenes feel brutal and real. cute game. arrived missing one piece but still can play and daughter loves it

I highly recommend this case. Especially if you use your phone for things like watching video, reading documents and such. The case is very protective at the corners which is where a phone gets most damage. The two features i love are the standing slot and the auto on. Whenever you open the case the screen automatically turns on. This feature doesnt waste any battery and it saves you if your power button is losing. you don't even have to latch it everytime, I only latch it when i put it in my pocket. Normally i just let the cover down until the latch is resting on the edge and it shuts the screen off. The inside is very soft and won't scratch the screen and the outside feels great. Nothing i can find wrong with it except i wish it covered the edges of the phone more like it does the corners.
Honestly better than I expected for how cheap it was. Works fine.

Michael G. Thomas is a well known author when it comes to science fiction novels and it definitely shows. He did really well with the third installment of the Star Crusades: Mercenaries series, "War of the Exiles". I was introduced to the series from a friend of mine that knows I am not a big sci-fi fan but after I read the first book - I just kept going. The book is a great example of a military sci-fi that puts you on a action packed journey full of surprises. The character development is top notch and the book is very well written. I personally fell in love with the character "Spartan" the most. The book has a ton of stuff going on including some terrorism which I believe is a way of connecting the reader to a more present state of whats currently going on around us in today's society. When you get into these books you will start noticing the author has a full on fictional universe going on inside his head not just a single story. I would compare it to something along the lines of Game of Thrones but in space with the depth in each series. Highly recommend the book to anyone who loves sci-fi / military books or just anyone looking for a great read in general.
Used these on my lathe because they are very hard to "bump" on and off by accident. They work great.

Took this on a three-day kayaking trip down the Delaware River and it performed flawlessly. My phone, wallet, and snacks stayed completely dry even after the bag got splashed repeatedly and took a plunge into the water when I capsized once. The closure system is super secure and gives me confidence that nothing will leak through. I also like that the fabric stays pliable instead of getting stiff and brittle like other dry bags I have owned. The attachment point worked great for clipping it to my kayak. For the price, you really cannot beat the quality. Highly recommend for anyone spending serious time on the water.

Got these for my Ruger air rifle and the consistency is impressive. Out of a tin of 500 I maybe found three deformed ones, which is way better than the cheaper bulk stuff I used before. Grouping tightened up noticeably at 30 yards compared to what I was shooting. They hit hard on pests and don't break the bank. My barrel likes the domed shape and I'll definitely keep a few tins stocked from now on.

I'll give it three stars because the cinematography is genuinely impressive. The way they shot the time loop sequences with those subtle visual cues in the background really caught my attention. Every time a reset happened, there were tiny changes in the lighting and set dressing that most people probably wouldn't even notice. That said, the story itself drags hard in the second act. The main character keeps making the same dumb decisions over and over and it gets frustrating watching him ignore obvious solutions. The supporting cast is fine but nobody really gets enough development to matter. It's the kind of movie that looks great on a nice TV but falls apart if you think about the plot for more than five minutes. Worth a rental if you're bored.

I'll be honest, I struggled to get through this one. The writing itself is perfectly competent, and I can see what the author was trying to do with these two couples and their decades-long connection. But nothing really happens? It's just page after page of quiet domestic moments and aging and reflecting on the past. I kept waiting for some kind of turning point or conflict to grab me, and it never came. That said, I think my mom's book club would eat this up. If you're someone who loves slow, literary character studies and doesn't need a plot to keep you turning pages, you might really connect with it. It's not badly written at all, it's just not for me. I need a little more tension to stay engaged.
This is a good buy for the money. Not the top of the line but just under it. I would recommend these to anyone. Definitely a good buy and fits perfectly.

I'd hands down recommend this to anyone who loves dark, gritty fantasy that doesn't wrap everything up with a neat little bow. The tension builds beautifully and that cliffhanger ending left me desperate for the sequel. Fantastic world building throughout.
Thought it'd be just another recipe collection, but the nutrition breakdowns are incredibly detailed. Way more thorough than I expected. Totally worth every penny.
Switched my two picky eaters to this and they lick the bowl clean every time. No tummy issues at all. Great stuff.

Arrived two days earlier than expected, which was a nice surprise. The packaging was a little flimsy, just a bubble mailer, but the transmitter itself was fine inside its retail box. First impression out of the package: this thing is tiny. Way smaller than the old clunky one I'm replacing. Snapped it right onto my iPod, no cables needed, and it paired up with my car stereo instantly. Signal strength seemed decent on my commute to work, definitely clearer than what I expected for something this small. Only knocked off a star because there's no pass-through charging while it's attached, so you're stuck draining your player's battery. Overall though, solid little gadget for the price and the fast shipping was appreciated.

I bought this for sanding drywall and staining decks, and it's way better than those cheap paper masks. The rubber seals against your face nicely without leaving marks, and the straps are easy to adjust. No dust or fumes get through at all. I wore it for about three hours straight doing epoxy work and forgot I had it on. If you do any woodworking, auto body work, or chemical projects around the house, grab one of these. Honestly worth every penny over disposable options. Already told two neighbors to get one.

Update: I received the 600 Professional today. First off, this filter is very large. It completely dwarfs both my Fluval 305 and Marineland 350 Magnum. I also notice that like my Fluval 305, the Hydor is manufactured in Italy. Something I find interesting, given all of the aquarium filters that are made in China. The 600 PRO is well constructed, with five media baskets: two for mechanical filtration and three for biological filtration. The motor housing for this filter is solid and well designed, and the motor itself is completely silent. The motor head is secured to the 600 PRO's canister with front and back clamps, as well as side levers which offer a tighter seal. Even the media trays have nice adjustable plastic straps which allow for easy removal of media, and the trays themselves fit snugly into the 600 PRO's chassis, stacked atop one another. Aesthetically, the entire Hydor Professional series has a refreshingly industrial look, which exudes quality. The fact that this filter was discounted by about $80 from its list price only makes the aforesaid that much better. If the Hydor 600 PRO stands up as well as my Fluval 305 and Eheim Classic filters have, I will consider this to be money well spent. Highly recommended! I have been considering purchasing one of these filters since they came out. However, they were selling at list price for quite awhile. So I decided to wait until the prices became more reasonable. I just found a brand new 600 for sale on Amazon for about $150, so I decided to purchase it. Will update my experiences. Based on what I have read about these filters thus far, I've left four stars. This is the largest canister filter I have ever purchased. I also own a Marineland Magnum 350 and a pair of Fluval 305s (350 GPH VS 260 GPH respectively), so at 345 GPH this will be about as large as the Magnum 350, even though the 600 dwarfs the 350 in size.

We used it for a party. After the party we put the sever in the refrigerator and used it a few days later for another party. I washed the unit by hand and washed the storage cups in the refrigerator. At first we kept the condiments cool with ice cubes and then used small ice packs.

I'm a huge fan of MC books but am not really that familiar with the world of BDSM so I was determined to read this book with an open mind. From the prolog B. b had me hooked to read the story of Colt and Tuesday. I loved that girls attitude from the get go, I loved the way they met and how their world's literally colieded. Their backstories are masterfully intertwined with their present and just made you want to be part of their journey even more and get to know them and the family that Colt introduces Tuesday to. Colts explanation of family to Tuesday is perfect: "You were raised believin' family is in your name - that it has something to do with blood. " "It has nothing to do with that s*** - that's all accidental b. s - the s*** your born into that you can't f***in' control. " "Family, are the people who are there for you, who've got your back, and you know dam good and well, you've got theirs too! You don't just run away from them!" Tuesday has some knowledge of Colts world but from a twisted dangerous point of view, he introduces her to a safe version of the world of BDSM, that has her wanting more. Along the way you learn how his bond with his brother Kash was formed, you learn how important trust can be and the lengths the brothers will go to, to protect their family. I've loved Colts journey so far and can't wait for his brother Kash' to begin.
Bought this for my son's birthday six months ago and it's still going strong despite being dropped and stepped on countless times. Great quality toy.
Arrived fast in secure mailer. Fun throwback flick for the collection.

Grabbed this on a whim during a sale and honestly it's worth way more than what I paid. Raine's journey from being bossed around to finally taking charge had me hooked by chapter two. Dax is exactly the kind of book boyfriend you want. The romance feels earned, not rushed. Already planning to re-read this one.

I was very excited to read this book. I have recently become the manager of a business and have been having a hard time trying to figure out how I should treat and deal with my staff. It's a small office and so we are have constant contact with each other and I have found it hard to strike the right balance between friend and boss. Many times I have felt uncomfortable when wanting to address something with one of my employees. This book has been invaluable to me in that it has given me a lot to think about and put into action. I like that there is a timeline so there are different questions to ask myself each day so that I just don't do a 180 in 24 hours. My employees may just walk out on me then! This book really made me identify to myself why I am the boss, what skills I possess to help this business and to take ownership of that. I would recommend this book to anyone who wants to work on their management skills! The author asked me to read and review this book and I am so glad he did. This is my honest review and I highly recommend the book.

Expected a lot more from this heater based on the description. I set it up in my 40 gallon turtle enclosure and right out of the box the temperature reading was all over the place. It would show 80 degrees one minute and then jump to 72 the next without actually heating the water consistently. My turtle seemed stressed from the fluctuations so I ended up swapping back to my old reliable aquarium heater. For the price I honestly thought this would be a solid upgrade but it feels cheaply made and the suction cups barely hold onto the glass. The digital display is nice in theory but pointless if the actual heating element can't maintain a steady temperature. Really disappointing given what was advertised.
Highly recommend for anyone who enjoys cozy Southern fiction. The narrator brings Miss Julia to life perfectly. Great for long road trips!
Quality is better than I expected for the price. Nice shirt. I bought "Best Dad Ever" for my son-in-law and "Best Kid Ever" in the same color for my granddaughter. They're a cute couple!
Bought this for my daughter whose name is Amber 2 Christmas' ago & she still wears it every day.
Wow, this book was excellent! Hated the fact that London Didn't go to Reese with her problem, but it all worked out! Must read, emotional, passionate, sexy read!!!!
Bought this for my daughter's birthday and she loves the makeup pieces. The small containers can be tricky for little hands to snap shut. Great gift for ages 4 and up!
Arrived two days early in sturdy retail packaging. Opened one right away to test, solid seal, no drips. Feels way better built than I anticipated.
Book arrived with a bent cover and creased pages. Looked used instead of new. Flipped through and the story seemed boring right away.

I bought this in blue as well. It fits well and has good weight without being overpowering or too clingy. I did buy it in small and I usually take an 8 but because it was very full, I thought the small would fit fine. It does.

Finally something that makes nail trimming stress-free. My golden retriever used to bolt the second she saw clippers, and I'd end up with crooked cuts and a dog who wouldn't come near me for hours. Switched to this Dremel last weekend and the difference is night and day. She actually sat still on the couch while I worked through all four paws. No splintering, no catching on the carpet afterward, and I could really smooth out the rough edges that clippers always leave behind. The variable speed is great because I started low to get her comfortable, then bumped it up once she relaxed. Battery held up through the full session with plenty left. Wish I had bought this years ago instead of fighting with regular clippers every month.

Paid maybe ten bucks for the paperback and honestly got more out of it than books triple the price. Manson cuts through all the fluff and just tells it straight. Read it twice already and keep finding stuff that sticks. Totally worth every penny.

Cute idea but honestly pretty flimsy. My adult cat sent the whole thing sliding across the floor on the first swat. The suction cup base barely holds on to anything and the dangling part came undone after maybe ten minutes of play. That said, my friend's tiny kitten loved it and was gentle enough that it stayed intact. If you have a young, lightweight cat who isn't aggressive with toys yet, this could be fine. For any cat with real power behind their paws, skip it.

Got this for my daughter's first birthday and she's now almost three. The concept is solid and she's learned a bunch of animal sounds and shapes from the books. My main gripe is how it's held up though. After about eight months of regular use, the pressure points on certain pages stopped registering touches consistently. We have to press really hard now or tap the same spot multiple times before it responds, which frustrates her to no end. The cartridge slot also feels flimsy, had to clean the contacts twice already because it would glitch out mid-story. The plastic shell itself is indestructible, I'll give it that. Survived being dropped on tile and chewed on. Just wish the internal touch sensors matched the build quality of the exterior.
Grabbed this on sale and it's worth ten times what I paid. Kristin Hannah nails the French resistance storyline. Stayed up way too late finishing it. Hardback quality is solid too.
Burned out within 1 month. Never even in rain.

Darkly comic tale with a revised film noir quality about two gangsters hiding out In Bruge located in a gray area somewhere between black and white that provides the back drop for an honesty of gangsters knowing their gangsters American audiences can appreciate

Ordered this for a trip to Costa Rica and it arrived two days earlier than expected. Box was well-protected with air pillows and the camera was snug inside its retail packaging. Popped in the SD card and battery, and it powered right up immediately. The build feels solid despite how light it is, and the screen is bright and crisp. Took a few test shots around the house and the color reproduction blew me away, especially for something this compact. Menu navigation is intuitive enough that I didn't need the manual. Really impressed so far and can't wait to put it through its paces on vacation.
Arrived next day in sturdy packaging. Cracked it open immediately and was hooked by page two. Absolutely worth every penny.

Watched this with my wife last weekend and we were both completely hooked from start to finish. The bull riding scenes are incredibly well done and you really get a sense of how dangerous and unpredictable the sport is. Luke Perry does a fantastic job bringing Lane Frost to life, capturing his genuine charm and determination. What surprised me most was how emotional the whole thing gets, especially toward the end. I actually grew up watching rodeo on weekends with my dad, so this hit close to home for me. The supporting cast is solid and the soundtrack fits perfectly without being overbearing. Grabbed the Blu-ray and the picture quality is sharp for an older film. Highly recommend it, whether you know anything about rodeo or not.

My initial impression of this shoe was that it was an over-priced, cheap shoe. I confirmed my suspicions about the price when I went to a local Nike outlet, only to find it 30.00 cheaper. Moreover, the sole wasn't cut in the same way of the original, i.e., in blocks intended to push apart to provide a natural shock impact. Too narrow inside and out. The sole outsole was wider in the original. Maybe just buying the name here.
Classic Eastwood. The desert cinematography is stunning and the pacing holds up perfectly. Worth every penny.

Great pocket kits that fit in my bag or any cargo pocket, plenty of storage for my ultra light tackle that comes with me daily (the joys of living next to a network of fish dense ponds and streams!) and has plenty of room for essentials. Mine have no problem with the latch keeping hold solidly, have dropped them on trails with no damage but again, these are small cases so I don't expect them to hold up to tumbles off a cliff.

Got this for my beagle and the quality is honestly solid. The leather feels genuine and the hardware seems like it'll hold up well over time. My main issue is the bulk, this thing is thick and pretty stiff right out of the package, so it took a couple weeks to break in. The adjustment slider could definitely be better designed too since I had to really wrestle with it to get the right fit. I'd recommend this for medium to large breeds with thicker necks. Toy or small dog owners should probably look elsewhere since the width overwhelms smaller frames. Decent collar overall, just not universal.

I have to put the disclaimer out there that we are not done training!! This is why I've only given 4 stars. I had to pause in the training as we went away (kitty went with) and didn't want to thoroughly confuse the poor guy! I have a young Maine Coon. He's approx. 17lbs. The CitiKitty has no trouble holding his weight. I considered toilet training after meeting a woman who has four cats and 1 bathroom. She and her husband trained their four cats to all use the toilet. It made me chuckle when she told us about it, but it stayed in the back of my mind. I got tired of stepping in litter - the Big Naughty is a slob! - and remembered the toilet training scenario mentioned above and decided to look into it. Week 1: The first week this worked really well. The Big Naughty didn't have any accidents. Week 2: After removing the inner ring, Naughty wasn't as big a fan of going on the toilet. He had a few accidents, and once he started going on the toilet again he decided he didn't like (or couldn't figure out) to go poo in the toilet; so he would go on the floor beside the toilet. As suggested we kept the ring at the week 2 mark for an extra week, but then I went away for the holidays and took him with me so I decided to put the training on hold until after we returned so as not to confuse him. I've yet to commence training again, I have quite a busy schedule. I am looking forward to starting again and completing the training. I dream of the day I do not step in the scattered litter on the bathroom floor, no longer having to sweep litter up daily, no longer buying litter, no more scooping litter, etc. I will update more thoroughly once we make the concerted effort to train again.

Been using this daily for about eight months now and it still looks practically new. I carry five cards and some cash in it every single day, and the leather hasn't cracked or stretched out at all. The clip still holds tight too, which surprised me. Got the brown one and the color hasn't faded from being in my pocket all day. Way better than my old bulky wallet. For the price this watch was great. It appears more expensive than it is. its a great looking sporty watch specially with the perforated band and yellow edges give a nice touch. Its really decent at removing paint and rust. Wears out of course, but for small jobs where you have rusted metal... great. I've used this for years on my front lights to welcome us home. In the driveway an infrared type light makes sense, but on the front lights using this timer in the automated setting is best. Installation: I fist use a contactless voltmeter to find the Hot Wire (has 120v current) hooked up to the light switch and mark it with nail polish. Then go turn off your circuit breaker. Next go an connect the BLACK wire to the one which you just marked with nail polish. You will need to find your neutral, which is generally white and NOT connected to a standard two pole light switch. Just find the neutral and some white 14 gauge solid white wire and create a pig tail; then connect it to the white wire with an electric wing nut. Lastly, connect the blue LOAD wire to the wire which was connected to your switch. Sometimes you might have a green wire which was a ground wire. I just loop it around the screw as I reinstall all the wires. I also tend to wrap everything in electric tape as I go to ensure no shorts anywhere. Go to google maps and get your current location, you will need the first two numbers of your Lattitude and Longitude. Press the big frond button for 3 seconds to enter configuration mode and follow the single directions. It's mostly updating the year, month and day, next the time and lastly the latitude and longitude. Lastly I don't like the back light on all the time, so I turn that off. It still glows for 8 seconds whenever you press any button. I use the defaul P01 which turn on at sunrise and off at 11pm in your time zone and automatically adjusts for day light savings time. This is a very well made unit. I've noticed they've been increasing in price but purchased an Amazon Warehouse deal which was perfect, never used but just broken packaging. It saved me a few bucks and worked perfectly.

I wasn't expecting much from this one, honestly picked it up free during a promo. But it actually turned out to be a really pleasant surprise.
