This book was romantic & fun. I enjoyed the story line about the Sinclairs & their scandal as well as the story of Colin & Della. To see them go from "enemies" to best friends to lovers was awesome. I liked that there was enough conflict to keep it interesting but not so much that *Spoiler alert* them getting back together wasn't inconceivable. Highly recommend this book & look forward to the next one.:-) *Given ARC for honest review through NetGalley

Bought this for a work conference dinner and it was perfect. The fabric has enough give to sit comfortably without wrinkling, and the cut is super forgiving around the midsection. I'm 5'6" and about 160 lbs, the medium hit right at the knee, which I thought was flattering without being too short for a professional setting. I'd highly recommend this to anyone who needs something polished for events where you want to look put-together but still be comfortable all evening. Washed mine on gentle and hung it to dry, no issues.

Very simple to set up. I liked the instruction booklet on how to use. I am training my dog not to eat washcloths. She pulls them off the towel rod and swallows them whole. Fortunately they do not go into the intestine, but stay in her stomach until she vomits them up. Just started using the Trainer by using the "beep". I lay towels around the house and use the "beep" and say "leave it" and she does not touch the towels. I even throw the towels in the air and she does not go for them. Next I will use the "shock" to reinforce. I always use a tiny treat when she responds positively. Eventually I hope to leave the bathroom doors open and the towels on the rods without her grabbing them. I highly recommend this system.

To be going through so much Im glad that Farrah prayers were finally answered. Caisen was god sent the way he stepped up and showed her different. Nick and Asia they rode hard for their family. Nia as the saying goes hurt people hurt people...
I counted down the days till this book came out. So worth the wait! And of course, this wonderful author leaves me wanting more.
Got these mounted in my pantry last weekend and they're incredibly sturdy. Perfect for anyone dealing with cramped kitchen storage. My neighbor already ordered a set after seeing mine.

I picked this up on a whim and ended up finishing it in two sittings. The story pulls you in right from the start and doesn't really let go. What hit me hardest was how believable the whole thing felt, the author clearly did their homework on how these operations actually work. Some parts were genuinely tough to read because of the subject matter, but that's kind of the point. Characters felt real, pacing was solid throughout. Definitely recommend if you want something gripping and eye-opening.

I would have bought two if they weren't almost $30 a piece. They are nice to have in the office when I sit in front of a computer all day. Two side notes: 1) Make sure you look around for a water bottle that is closer to the length of the "wick". A normal.9fl oz (500ml) bottle is too tall for the wick to reach the bottom or at least closer to it so you have to fill the bottle up before it gets to 3/4 of the way empty. 2) If you don't use it everyday or even occasionally just from going home from work and coming back, the silver piece (metal diaphragm vibrating at an ultrasonic frequency) at the top of the humidifier gets "stuck" and I have to kind of poke at it to get it running or shake the bottle up. It kind of freaks me out that its broken but it starts right up after doing that. Not a big deal as long as it always works!

I rented this video from the library and as soon as I was finished, I came onto Amazon and ordered it. I like Chris Freytag, she is a good instructor and she is upbeat. I like the different combinations(I especially like playing catch in one of the segments) I worked up the sweat that I am used to working up.

Who knew that our kids would want to be retro and listen to old vinyl records? When we bought our daughter a record player, we realized she couldn't listen to our old 45 speed vinyl singles on it. This little adaptor did the trick. This makes more sense than those individual adaptors that have to be inserted into each individual disc. With this, you just put it on the player and you can plan one 45 after another, then remove it to listen to albums.

Bought these for a wedding and ended up wearing them all weekend. The heel height is spot on for someone who can't do tall shoes anymore but still wants to look put together. No breaking in needed, no blisters, and they actually stayed comfortable through hours of dancing. Total win.

I had a chance to listen to these at a local audio shop. I wasn't too impressed at all. Really disappointing sound quality considering the price. The mids are what's really bad I think--they have a tin-can quality to them that's not pleasant or realistic. This is common in the Beats line, I think the Studios are slightly better but they have other issues. For the same price or less, you could get some really great headphones. Check out AKG's Q-701, or Sennheiser Amperiors, or Audio-Technica M50's, or Shure 840's. They're all much more realistic sounding with clearer mids, smooth highs, and tight realistic bass. You'll learn to love and appreciate the more realistic sound other headphones provide, and your wallet will appreciate it too.
I love this cup! It's well made and the color is excellent. Perfect for my coffee in the morning surrounded by all my little owls in the sun room!:)
Not sure what the critics are talking about because I like this movie. Moves fast good story line but it is a lot like another movie I have. I just like Tatum Channing.

This is a great family game or a fun party game. We received it as a Christmas gift from my in laws and it was quickly a family favorite. It's fun just for me and my husband to play or for the whole family to join in.

Bought this a few weeks back and it's been a game changer for my grocery runs. The divider cards are awesome because I personalized them with my own categories instead of sticking to the pre-printed ones. Fits perfectly in the child seat area of the cart so it stays out of the way while I shop. I'd definitely recommend this to anyone trying to get organized, especially busy parents or meal preppers. Honestly, my impulse buys have dropped significantly since I started using it. Planning to grab another for my mom!
excellent reading and story. Could not put the book down.

I was pretty skeptical about buying a bed in a box, especially at this price point, but I'm glad I took the chance. Unrolled it onto my frame and let it breathe for the afternoon. By evening it was fully expanded and ready to go. I've had it about three weeks now and my back pain has basically disappeared. The medium firmness hits that sweet spot where you sink in just enough but still feel supported. My husband runs hot and he hasn't complained about overheating once. For what we paid, I honestly expected something flimsy or cheap feeling. This honestly rivals the $1,200 mattress we replaced. If you're on the fence, just go for it. Absolutely worth every penny.

Poor installation, did not apply well. Lots of bubbles all throughout the screen. I ordered another product from a different company and had to just take this one off and throw it away. Just skip it and buy a different one, I recommend Coolreall
This faucet was easy to install and looks great. Our old faucet was leaking and just looked old, this one works great, love it.

Got this crate eight months ago for my 60 pound pit bull and it's held up incredibly well. She used to paw at the bars constantly during crate training and nothing has bent or loosened. The metal is genuinely sturdy and the latch mechanism still works smoothly. I really like having the divider since I could make the space smaller when she was a puppy. Folds flat too which helped when we moved last month.
Bought this for my night shifts and morning jogs. The fit is super comfortable and doesn't ride up at all. Definitely grabbing a second one soon.

Really wanted to like this lamp since the color-changing LED modes are actually super cool and the design looks great on my nightstand. But the clock is completely useless. Every time I plug my phone into the USB port on the base, the display glitches out and resets to 12:00. Tried three different cables and it still happens. For something that sits on a nightstand, not being able to charge your phone without killing the clock is a dealbreaker. Returned it after a week.
Arrived two days early, boxed flat with no creases. Pulled it out, popped it right into the slot. Perfect fit, solid quality.
Love Lindsays work, it is writted with a lot of class and a real page turner again and again. I can't wait for more.

All I wanted was something to tell me how many watts my appliances are consuming. I can figure out the cost myself. But it won't tell me what the wattage consumption is. Need to plug it in for a kilowatt hour or so. And how do I translate that back to wattage? Way too complicated, and misses what I had hoped was its basic purpose. Sending it back.
I sat through the entire run of Angel but this is rough going. Maybe you have to have a Valley Girl mentality.
Its so fun to watch my kids engage with this DVD and learn another language. It works amazingly and they are learning to talk with there hands
Love it! Sturdy. Beautiful. Comes pretty darn sharp, but I plan on getting it just a bit more 'razor's-edge'. Nice leather sheath (which I plan on waterproofing).

For the price, I honestly wasn't expecting much, but this thing has been a great addition to my garage gym. I was paying $40 a month for a spin class membership, so this paid for itself in just a few weeks. Assembly took maybe 30 minutes and the tools are included. The resistance is smooth and consistent, and the seat doesn't bother me even on longer rides. My wife and I both use it daily and the adjustable settings accommodate our height difference easily. If you're on a budget but still want a solid cardio option, don't hesitate on this one.

Nice and simple pot. Has six hour timer, however, would like to have a longer delay for the day use. Also, when using the timer, it always resets the final temperature to 190 degree. Would be nice if it can "remember" the previous temperature setting. The inner container is coated with floury charcoal, not teflon coating that any other brands offer. It claims to bring out the fine taste of water. To me, at least it looks better than the grey teflon coating. Another feature missing is it does not come with the audible alarm. Extra spec that I would like to know before ordering: Power: 700 Watt Temp setting: 140, 180, 190, 208 No audible alarm for any events. After all, I am happy with the pot for the price and the build quality. Not sure if I would pay extra $30+ for the fancy features Zojirushi brand offers.

Ordered this on my Kindle last night and it downloaded instantly, no issues at all. The cover caught my eye right away and the formatting was clean throughout, no weird gaps or typos like you sometimes get with digital books. Started reading during my lunch break and honestly could not put it down. The chemistry between the main characters is off the charts from literally the first chapter. Nolan is exactly the kind of intense, over-the-top protective hero that keeps me up way past my bedtime. Chloe holds her own too which I appreciated. For a shorter read it still packs a serious punch. Already went and bought the next book in the series. If you like your romance heroes possessive and a little unhinged, grab this one immediately.
Worth every penny. Keeps my water ice-cold all day at work and the powder coat hasn't chipped after months of daily use. Great investment.
The engravings are absolutely beautiful. Inside is lined with a red velvet material. Perfect sized for rings.
Bought these as a gift for my brother-in-law who loves bourbon. He was thrilled. Solid feel, classy look. Perfect for anyone who enjoys a good cocktail.
Exactly as described and replaced our old door time perfectly.
Well this is the second one, the first was returned due to a leak. Thinking that everyone as a defect here and now, and give it another try. This one the LED does not work!!!

Got these for a formal event last month and was honestly bracing for the worst since control top anything usually digs into my stomach and makes me miserable all night. These were a pleasant surprise. The waistband sits comfortably without rolling down, and the thickness is just right, not so sheer that they rip instantly, but not heavy enough to make you sweat. I'm 5'7 and around 160 lbs, and the large fit perfectly with no weird sagging at the ankles or tugging at the crotch. Wore them for a solid eight hours of walking, sitting, and dancing and they didn't slip once. Survived the wash on delicate too. Already ordered a second pair in black for work. Definitely my go-to from now on.

For the price, this whisk can't be beat. The construction is solid, and it handles everything from pancake batter to heavy bread dough without bending. Comfortable grip too. Way better than the flimsy ones at the grocery store.

When I saw this offered on Vine, I wasn't sure how sturdy this would be. It arrived yesterday and let me tell you, it is definitely stronger than it looked! This "toolbox" could be used for a variety of things. Besides a step stool, you've got an extra seat. There are no rollers on it so it will stay put. The storage inside is divided into 3 removable trays. You can put a lot of tools, gardening equipment, etc. inside. My favorite part is the removable power cord with 4 outlets. The cord is 20 feet long on a wind up wheel with the 4 outlets on the outside of the wheel. I'm sure this toolbox will be around for a long time.
Been wearing these almost daily for three months and they still look great. Super solid build.
Perfect for fans of quirky middle-grade humor. I'd hand this to any kid who loves Lemony Snicket or teachers reading aloud to a classroom. Fun London setting too.
Worth every penny and then some. Backman's storytelling grabs you right away and doesn't let go. I stayed up way too late finishing this. Buy it.

Took a 70lb pig this past January 2017..they fly pretty straight almost took the head off a squirrel at 30yrds,he ducked at the last second..As to the cutting abaility i cant really say since i hit the pig in the eye and arrow traveled straight into the brain. Got these for my daughter's first trip to the shooting range and they worked perfectly. Fit her head snugly without being uncomfortable, and the sound dampening was impressive for the price. Solid quality construction.

Got this for my daughter's birthday and honestly I was skeptical about how well it would actually function. Most Play-Doh toys promise the world and deliver a mess. Surprisingly, the cyclone mechanism cranks out little candy shapes pretty consistently when you load it right. My kids spent a solid hour making gummy bears and lollipops without getting bored, which is rare in our house. The downside is there are tons of tiny pieces that end up scattered everywhere. We started keeping them in a ziplock bag because the box doesn't hold anything securely. Also expect to do some scraping to get dried dough out of the small crevices. Overall though, it delivers on what it advertises and the kids genuinely love it. Would recommend for ages 4 and up.
I love these. Went down a half a size and it's perfect.

Arrived quickly but the box was pretty beat up and the container had shifted around loose inside. No cracks though, so that's something. First impressions are decent, feels a little flimsy but the lid fits snug. Haven't tested it yet but it seems like it'll do the job for quick pasta nights.

The Silver magic series is perhaps my favorite by this author. And this second book is the natural follow-up to the first. Everyone's favorite Dees from the first book, Glass, here has her chance to try life as a human. And she quickly lands herself in a heap of trouble, along with a romantic intrigue. This book features the same imaginative inventiveness as the first book, and the same colorful characters. The Dees are wonderful as always. But the author's most brilliant characters, in my mind, are the Brothers Grimm. metaphysical hit men who live by their own convoluted code. This pair is calling for their own book. But let's get back to the book at hand, which is grand enough in it's own right. I don't want to reveal too much, but if you thought the Dees were magnificent in defense of the Howard mansion, just wait until you see them turned loose in this book.

I really enjoyed the small-town restaurant setting in this book. The author clearly knows her way around a kitchen because the food descriptions alone made me hungry reading it. Cathy is a relatable main character and her reluctance to get involved with the Hollywood crowd felt genuine instead of forced drama. I appreciated that the romance built slowly instead of rushing into things. My one complaint is that the ex-boyfriend subplot kind of gets dropped halfway through and never fully resolves. Felt like a loose end that needed tying up. The chemistry between Cathy and the TV producer is great though and their banter had me smiling. Quick engaging read perfect for a weekend afternoon. Would definitely pick up the next book in the series.
Got this for my toddler before the new baby arrived. He loves the little matching game inside. Keeps him busy while I nurse!

Picked this up on a whim expecting just another quick card game to pass the time, but it's become our go-to family night choice. The elimination aspect worried me at first, but rounds wrap so quickly that nobody's sitting out long. My six year old picked up the strategy after one round and now regularly beats the adults. Surprisingly deep for something this compact. Definitely exceeded what I thought we were getting.

Bought a pair of these for my dual sport riding out in the desert. After eight months of rattling around on my rack through whoops and rocky washes, they haven't cracked or leaked a drop. The cap seals tight and the mounting brackets held firm even when I laid the bike down on a tricky single track. Knocked off one star because the included hardware felt a little cheap so I swapped it out for stainless bolts. Solid fuel backup that takes a beating.
Perfect for my mobility scooter trips to the pharmacy. Highly recommend to anyone needing extra cargo space.
Perfect gift for any one-year-old's birthday party.
For the price it's hard to complain, but I had to sand down the edges to make it work. Kids still enjoy it though.

Loved the movie, but it left a bad (director (Besson), and so called visual effects) after taste. The first BIG downer, was when Lucy was repeatedly kicked in the stomach by a drug czars henchmen, when they knew the very expensive drugs where inside her belly; REALLY? The next blunder was on the part of the so-called visual effects people (there were a few of them to blame), when they used the old roll the room effect that is so old and lame, that nobody uses it anymore. REALLY? I found the effects (or the lack of) the most offensive part of the movie, especially the growing black computer fingers; REALLY? Not a bad movie with a 40mil budget/88 min running time, and I am sure Scarlett and Morgan got a large share of it, as it was not well spent on the production. Compare this with the 2012 The Avengers, at 220mil/143 min running time, and you can see it was a low budget film from the start, and for that reason, they actually did a pretty good job.

Expected way more control for walks. My labrador still drags me down the street, the front clip barely makes a difference. The padding seemed decent but shifts around constantly and ends up sitting right on her shoulders weird. Honestly my old ten dollar harness worked about the same.

Great for hiding the cash and cards that we need to have on us but don't want people to see. Now just have to remember to go to a quiet and private place to use it instead of showing bad guys what we have and where we have it.

I had first purchased a much smaller bag thinking that I kid meet my needs, but it wasn't even large enough to hold two charging cords. The depth of this bag allows me to bring all related cords for my three devices with me when I travel.
Been using this on my two dogs for about six months now and the bottle is still going strong. The pump hasn't clogged once and the formula works just as well as day one. Definitely repurchasing.

I ordered this dress with a little worry over how it would fit and if the color would match the picture. I was not disappointed on either account. It is flattering and fits me just right but since it is flowy and has a little stretch, I don't have to worry about feeling uncomfortable if I gain or lose a couple lbs before I have to wear this dress out. I do agree with other reviewers that I would not go braless in this as it is a bit low-cut but the design of the sleeves and back make this not a problem as a bra can easily be worn without showing. I am very pleased with this purchase!

Expected more emotional depth given the premise. The protagonist's coping mechanism was interesting but never fully explored. A decent read for book club discussion, but nothing that stayed with me after finishing. Short enough to get through in a weekend.

Bought this for my dad who runs hot at night and refuses to sleep in anything heavy. He's 5'10" around 200 lbs and the L/XL drapes nicely without being bulky. Hits mid-thigh on him. Would definitely recommend to anyone looking for a breathable robe for warm climates or hot sleepers. Great value too.

I returned the game because it was poorly made. The holes that were supposed to be in the center of the discs were off-center, so they didn't stack properly. Some of the holes weren't cut big enough, so they didn't even fit on the pegs. The pegs don't fit well into the holes that were cut for them, so they fall out easily while the game is being played.
This is so cute.i bought it for my 15 month old son and he loves playing with it.no leak so perfect
Great bright targets for the money. Spotting shots at 20 yards was a breeze.

Seagull 1x-2x Right Angle Finder for Canon, Nikon, Pentax, Minolta, Fuji, Olympus and Leica SLR cameras is very high quality and it obtains very good image quality. I recommend this product for DSLRs.

I had been searching for one of these locally for awhile. Finally picked one up here on Amazon. I am not sure how I lived without one before. I have not had a single boil over with this. It does rattle in the pot. In my case I like the rattle as I can tell if the water is boiling. It washes up really easily in the sink. It's a little bit of a pain to fish it out of whatever you are cooking. Even so, it's worth the effort.

Bought this for a Christmas gift for a guy, that has everything., hoping that this will get him hooked on the series., All of Foyle's War Series is wonderful, and also, period history., you will enjoy it!!!!! Enjoy, Paula

Love having the zipper. Still comfortable as regular scrub pants but more durable and easy to get in and out of. (Not having to worry with a knot in the drawstring.) Was told at a local Scrub store that Dickey is discontinuing this style. I hope they change their mind because they are great for men.
Have always used Tachikara since middle school and have remained a faithful user since. The leather is soft and doesn't hurt your hands. Love the various colors it comes in!
Perfect for anyone with an active job. Sturdy fabric, true to size, and holds up after washing.

Stop your search for a bike light and just buy this one. It's perfect. Previously, I had been using two headlights at once (a Planet Bike 2 Watt Blaze and a Bell Lumina) which works good but with the Cygolite you only need one light and it's totally worth the $80.. The brightest setting is actually too bright. I can't imagine a situation where you would need a light that bright. When you turn on the light it defaults to the second brightest setting which makes sense because this is the one you'll use when it's totally dark. As the sun comes up I switch to the next lowest setting. The flashing mode is great for dawn and dusk but be warned it's quite bright and you may severely annoy approaching motorists (which isn't necessarily a bad thing). To change levels you just click the big (and only) button conveniently located on the top of the light. To turn the light on or off you just hold this button down. The mount on this light is fantastic. I much prefer the thumb screw that this uses as opposed to the mount other lights use. In fact that was a selling point for me on this light and it's why I chose it over the NiteRider MiNewt 350. You can get the light on the bike nice and tight. Another nice feature is that the light can tilt up or down. This light is rechargeable which is the way to go. As long as you always have have a full charge you know you'll have a bright light. But make sure you charge it regularly because when it runs out of juice it shuts off. The battery is also simple to remove. The light feels solid and it's withstood a light rain. I'm going to buy another mount so that I can use this light on more than one bike.

Honestly didn't expect such a small part to make this much of a difference. I've been struggling with the factory slide lock on my Glock 19 Gen 4 since day one. My fingers aren't huge, but catching that tiny nub to pull the slide back was always a hassle, especially at the range when my hands were sweaty. I figured this upgrade might help a little, but it completely solved the problem. The extended portion sticks out just right, enough to get a solid grip without snagging on holsters or clothing. Field stripping is finally quick and painless. Installation took about five minutes with a punch and a YouTube video. Way better than I anticipated for the price. Should've bought this months ago.

They look tough and I may be a special case... I've had these got less than a month an use then when I ride my Harley... They are plugged into my phone sitting in my pocket. Occasionally I lean or put pressure on the jack, an now they are losing connection when moved so they are no longer useful in situations where my phone is getting moved around.

Got these for a formal event and they did not disappoint. The quality is way better than what I paid, they easily pass for a much pricier brand. Fit true to size and no blisters after hours of dancing. Couldn't be happier with this purchase for the money.
I have this in the wall in my therapy office and my clients love it!!! Perfectly covered a nasty old wall!!

This yoga mat by Heart Centered Yoga LLC is great for my 21 Day Fix program that I'm doing. It's a thin mat compared to some that I have seen, but it's made of great quality materials and protects my body when I am down on the ground exercising. It adds just the right amount of support and comfort so I am able to perform my exercises on the hard wood floor without worrying about my knees or back being in pain. It's made of 1/4" memory foam and so it's super comfortable when you lay on it. It provides enough grip to the floor and enough stability to your feet that you won't slip on it. The mat itself looks really nice. I bought it in black and it has a nice textured look to it that feels great when you lay on it There were a few other color choices, but I'm glad I went with the black so you don't see any dirt or sweat on it. If it does get sweaty, which it usually does, you can just wipe it down with your towel, and then wash it down with a mild soap and hang it to dry, very simple. The size of the mat itself is about a typical yoga mat I would say, it's long enough for me to stretch out on it and still be off the floor. It measures 24" x 72". It also comes with a strap that you can put around each side once it's rolled up so you can carry it on your back.
Bought this for my husband's work lunches and it's been great. Keeps everything cold till noon and holds plenty of food. Really solid purchase.

As a former sporting goods purchasing agent for Kmart about 15-16 years ago - when the first mass production of metal scooters were being imported from China - I can say that I've seen a few scooters in my lifetime. Some of those sample scooters lasted me until today - scooters my kids can still enjoy these days. This is a scooter with a great concept for power - it's all manual yet very simple to operate. The one big issue I have with this item that otherwise claims to hold riders up to 90 kg =200lbs(surprisingly, this is the same weight limit as the jr. model, which is aimed at 4-8 year olds) is the following: the plastic enclosure at the bottom of the scooter, the part that holds a wire and is close to the ground seems extremely fragile. I venture to say if a kid rides on the sidewalk and then decides to go jump on the street, that plastic piece could easily get smashed in the sidewalk corner, and render the entire scooter useless.

Paying for college is a pain. To help with money she doesn't have she does dancing or rather stripping at night. Alex is a friend of hers who only protects the girls and bounces people out of the club. She is drawn to him but doesn't bother because she doesn't want her eyes clawed out by his gf. When sh@t hits the stage or rather blood does. What's a girl to do with a big secret she had to carry for her and Alex over their heads. Will they make it out of this alive or will be found at the bottom of a lake.
Arrived in two days with no bent corners. Skimming through it, looks super helpful so far.

Picked this up on sale and honestly it's worth what I paid. The leads have decent chemistry and the story kept me watching, though nothing blew me away. For a budget-priced DVD, the picture quality is fine. Don't expect cinematic greatness, but it passes the time on a slow weekend afternoon.

I bought 3 of these - One small, one large, and one XL. The large and XL fit my husky and Pit bull perfectly, but the small one, for my small 12 lb bichon mix was way too tight. Maybe this is my misinterpretation, however, I think most folks would think a Small would fit a 12 lb dog w/ a small head and neck. Other than the fact that I think it is mislabeled, (and the fact that the jingling bells will drive anyone crazy after a few minutes), I would give this item 5 stars. As an avid cube puzzle lover who has owned many cubes in his 10+ years of cubing, I have to say that this 4x4x4 is amazing. This cube turns very quickly, smoothly, allows easy corner cutting, and overall feels really comfortable in your hands. It has the same color scheme as the original Rubik's brand 4x4x4 (although the Moyu orange is a slightly different shade) so it is a very easy transition from a Rubik's brand cube. I don't feel like I have to worry about pieces popping out with the Moyu cubes, even when turning the sides very quickly, something that often happens with the Rubik's brand cube. I highly recommend this cube to anyone looking to get a 4x4x4.
Was exactly how it was described. Will be ordering again.
Expected this to hold up way better given the price. My four-year-old snapped a piece off within the first hour of normal play. Super flimsy and not worth the money at all.

I'd guess this is over-priced compared to what I could get at a hardware store, but it's high quality. For the project I was working on, it was far more convenient to have it delivered. After a few months of regular reading, the binding already started splitting and pages are coming loose. The theological content is pretty thin too, lots of vague speculation without solid backup. Expected way more substance for the price. Disappointing quality all around.

Got this for my mother's apartment since she didn't want to drill into her fiberglass surround. The mounting tape holds surprisingly well, no slipping after three weeks of daily showers. Just make sure you clean the surface with alcohol first and pick your spot carefully, because once that adhesive grabs, it's not coming off without a fight.

I stayed up way too late finishing this one. The chemistry between Amber and Lance is electric from the very first chapter. What I really loved was the push and pull dynamic, she doesn't just cave immediately, and Lance has to actually work for it. The tension builds in a way that feels natural, not forced. The dialogue is easily the standout feature for me. It's smart and playful without trying too hard. I caught myself grinning like an idiot at some of their exchanges. The pacing is solid too, no weird lulls in the middle where nothing happens. My only tiny complaint is I wish it were longer because I wasn't ready to say goodbye to these characters. Already checking if there's a sequel.

Got this for walking my golden and the built-in bag dispenser is honestly genius. No more stuffing plastic bags in my pockets. The retractable mechanism works fine most of the time but occasionally locks up when you're trying to reel your dog back in quickly. Not a dealbreaker though. I'd definitely recommend this to anyone who does daily neighborhood walks and always forgets to grab bags on the way out the door.
Took this out on the lake last weekend and was honestly impressed by how stable it feels. The molded seats are super comfortable for long paddles. Fits perfectly in my SUV too.
Quality product that is an easy install and works as it should; nothing else much to say.
Got this on sale and honestly for the price you can't complain. Fit is definitely snug so I'd suggest sizing up. Material feels decent and it's held up fine through a couple workouts so far.
Watched this five times already and the disc still plays perfectly. Great thriller that holds up on repeat viewings. Totally worth buying.
Bought these for my nieces and they haven't taken them off since. Great material and the colors are vibrant.

Whether this lasts a long time or not I do not know, but it is an attractive soap dish. Watched this with my 10-year-old last weekend. It opened up conversations I'd been putting off for months. He actually asked questions afterward instead of tuning out. Every parent with kids online needs this.

I picked this up on a whim and ended up reading the whole thing in one sitting. The chemistry between the main characters is fantastic, you really feel the tension every time they're on the page together. What I liked most is that Sierra isn't just some passive heroine waiting to be rescued. She actually takes control of her life and makes tough choices, even when the stakes are high. The island setting is gorgeous and the scuba diving details feel authentic without slowing the story down. There's real emotional depth here too, the family dynamics hit close to home for me. If you want a romance with actual substance behind the swoony moments, this one delivers. Already checking out other books by this author.

I'm pretty impressed with this litter trap mat. The material is spongy and soft for the cat to walk on and it's big enough to cover the entrance to our litter box while still providing a lot of cover on either side and in front so the cat can't just jump over it on the way out. The spacing of the material is wide enough so that it really can be vacuumed and shaken out (the previous litter trap mat we had was made from a similar material but once litter got in there it was very hard to get out even after vigorous shaking or vacuuming). It's also a little thicker so that it doesn't feel flimsy. I've had some half-circle litter trap mats and they seemed like a good idea but left too much space on the sides of the entrance. This one covers everything. I really like this thing. Some reviewers mentioned a strong plastic smell when opening this and that's true but it's not a big deal. It's a mat that sits outside your cat's litter box, so plastic odor is not a big deal. It's definitely not perfect and doesn't catch all litter, but with good positioning it captures most and that perfect product that does it all hasn't been invented yet.

Bought these for a weekend trip to Vermont and they completely exceeded my expectations. The anti-fog coating is the real standout here, I was skiing hard in wet snow conditions and never once had to pull them off to wipe the lenses clear. My old pair from a big-name brand would fog up constantly, so this was a huge upgrade. The field of vision is also fantastic, with zero distortion around the edges. They fit comfortably over my prescription glasses without pinching, which was a relief. The strap adjusted easily and stayed put even on rough mogul runs. For the price, I honestly expected them to feel cheap, but the build quality is solid and they look way more expensive than they actually were. Definitely recommending these to friends.
it works great with my d3200 and i really needed it so this thing will hopfully hold up to the test

Bought this three weeks ago and use it every morning. Pretty quiet compared to my old one and cleans up fast. If you're someone who juices daily, this is absolutely worth it. Great for beginners who don't want to spend a fortune either.

I honestly didn't expect much from these glasses, figured the measurement lines would be cheesy or look like a measuring cup. But I was really surprised. The lines are etched into the glass in a way that just looks like a nice decorative pattern. You'd never know they were portion markers unless someone pointed it out. I've been trying to stick to a single glass of wine a night instead of accidentally pouring half the bottle, and these actually keep me honest without feeling like I'm on some kind of diet. The glass itself feels sturdy too, not thin or fragile like a lot of wine glasses I've ordered online. They've gone through the dishwasher a bunch of times and the markings haven't faded. Way better quality than the price suggests.

I'm not surprised I enjoyed this story so much or that I read it so fast. It only took me what seemed like an hour and some change to read the book. I was intrigued by the characters and their stories. I really liked how honest Kyra was about the problems former addicts face. The dynamic between Ellie, Drew, and Jason was very interesting. I felt bad for her being caught between two of the most important men in her life. I found Jason and Drew's interactions to be fascinating. There is a lot of history there and it made for some tense moments as well as emotional ones. However, only part of it was shared and I'm hoping we'll get more if/when we get Jason's story. I'm really looking forward to seeing this series develop and getting to know more about the band members and their stories. Kyra has written an emotional story that shows she's done a lot of research. It's one I thoroughly enjoyed reading.

The variable speed setting is genius, the slower speed is perfect for my older lazy cat, and my younger one goes nuts on the higher setting. Battery life has been solid so far, running almost daily for three weeks on the same set. It's quiet enough to have on while watching TV. Great buy for the price, highly recommend.

Literally chucked in rucksacks on hiking trips, read in the bath, left out in the garden, and the spine is still holding up fine. That said, Kelk's writing style is still fun and breezy, and the romance elements are sweet when they actually happen. I'd recommend this to anyone who loved the first book and wants to continue the series, or fans of Sophie Kinsella looking for something light for a beach read. WOW. Great book. I have family in Haifa, Israel. Just after the Hezbollah bombings there they showed me areas that had been bombed. These areas were just a few short miles from my family's home. I read this book with my eyes as wide as plates. It is a sweeping trip through many of the capitals of the Holy Lands and cradle of civilization. It is also very sobering. We are talking about a very rough "neighborhood" when we talk of the Middle East. This book does a magnificent job of bringing these complicated disputes into brilliant focus. It was the title of the book that caught me. The content is what leads me to a FIVE STAR review. Great writing from page one.

Sub Sports has my outdoor-compression-layer business for life! I have arctic compression leggings from other big-names, and these blow them out of the water! The fit is perfect, compression is even and full-length. They feel like second skin, breathe well, wick moisture well, yet retain heat like a wool blanket. These are better than Mil-spec ECW poly-pro, even with a base-layer added! They also don't have a strong rubber smell, nor do they retain odors like some of the other brands do. They do tend to ride a bit high on the waist if you opt for a larger than necessary size (looser fit), so be aware of sizing before purchasing. I used the size charts on the Sub Sports website, and got a perfect fit. If you are an athletic-build, or even average, then follow their sizing and you can't go wrong. If you are muscular-bulky or have a few extra pounds, go up a size for a better or looser fit. I also got the zip-top to match, and following the size chart would have put me in a large, but my son has this in a large (which is too short/tight in the sleeves for me), so I opted for the XL for better fit in my shoulder/arm length (I have broad sholders and 34-35" sleeves), and the compression is still nice and snug top-to-bottom. A word of warning... do NOT DRY THESE IN THE DRYER, or you'll never fit a toe back into them (or they will rip apart)... they must be line-dried or hung from a hanger. They dry quickly so don't think it will take all day. I wore a pair of these leggings kayaking last spring, and even though water was constantly getting me wet, within a few minutes of getting out of the kayak, I felt dry at the skin, and was NOT cold the whole time.

Very solid and durable cable, which allowed me to easily run my surround sound speakers. Nine years ago, Alex Delaware successfully treated Melissa Dickinson, a tormented and phobic young girl, irrationally scared of almost everything. After two years of treatment, Melissa seems almost totally recovered, so her need for Dr Delaware ceases, and she becomes one of his most spectacular triumphs. Now, Melissa contacts Alex again, this time seeking advice concerning her mother. Gina Dickinson is a recluse, an ex-actress hiding away from the world ever since a vicious acid attack that left her scarred for life, even after extensive and traumatic plastic surgery. Even though Gina is now seeing, with some effect, a psychiatrist of her own, Melissa wants to know if Alex feels her mother could cope if she went away, accepting her place Harvard. Then, one day, Gina inexplicably climbs into her car, and drives off into thin air, leaving a tangled mystery to be unravelled in her wake. I had started to think that this series was in danger of going stale. The prose is adequate and easy to read, but hardly full of spirit and at times seems a little perfunctory, and Alex Delaware has also remained a rather static - if very likeable - character. But now, after reading Kellerman's excellent standalone "The Butcher's Theatre", I returned to the series with "Private Eyes", and found it a wonderfully invigorating experience. This may be his lone of his longest Delaware books to date, but every word is fascinating, and there seems to me to be fresh fire in the writing. The characters are all very well developed, and although Kellerman never really takes any risks with his well-structured plot, it's a complex and clever book that really kicks the brain into gear, and presents one or two nice surprises along the way. The psychology is dead-on, the relationships are all fascinating, the characterisation is acute, and the resolution is exciting, well-done, and satisfying. This may well turn out to be the rock of the Delaware series. To find out, i shall have to read on...

The idea, conceived by producer J. J. Abrams, is brilliant, and the story told here is exciting, riveting stuff. It's tense and, at times, genuinely scary. If not for one little problem, I would have thoroughly enjoyed this film. The problem is in the presentation. Since the entire movie is supposed to come from a single hand-held camera, everything is in Shaky-Cam (or, as Roger Ebert aptly called it, "Queasy-Cam"). Hence, the movie quickly and continually induces vertigo in its audience; of the six members of my party, four reported some degree of motion sickness. Somewhere further down in the theater, we heard at least one person vomit. No matter how good a movie is -- and this one is, in its way, very very good -- it suffers when you can't bear to look at the screen. It's hard to appreciate the filmmaker's craft when you're trying to keep down the buffalo chicken sandwich you foolishly ate on the way to the theater. by Tom Knapp, Rambles.
