Well, you know what will happen by reading the blurb but not exactly all the particulars, some of which are fun and interesting. No real unicorn here, of course, but the symbolism plays a part in the story. There are also some entertaining secondary characters, although nobody is drawn with much dimension. However, this is not Chater's best work. I did not like the hero much. Too immature, too erratically friendly and then cold and almost mean to the heroine. Still, the writing is good. Chater knows her way around words and sentences, but I have to like the hero in my romance to be completely satisfied.

Wow. Our PetSmart trainer actually recommended this one and what a terrible idea that was. First I'd like to mention that it does not come with the batteries so don't forget to buy those. Actually, don't buy those because this will probably go right into the garbage. The noise is audible to people. Barely, but you can hear it. The noise is really high-pitched and irritating so instead of making my dog shut up, it just made him bark louder. You know how most dogs are with vacuum cleaners? That's how my dog is with the Bark-Off. He'll chase after it and bark bark bark until I turn it off. I tried leaving him alone with it on and it only agitated him further and made his anxiety worse. Save your couple bucks for something else.

Expected these to be lighter based on the product photos, but they've got some real heft to them. That said, the construction feels solid and they've handled rocky trails well after a few weeks. Sizing was tricky, glad I went up a half size. Not as nimble as trail runners, but great support for day hikes. Decent value overall.
my 7 year old got a ton of use in the snow and mud. never leaked, no cold feet.

First, this toy is far too large, even at the shortest placement of the seat. My kid is TALL for her age and she can barely reach the pedals. Second, the turning radius is awful. We have a huge driveway and this thing can't turn around. You have to either back it up and do a three point turn, or get off, pick it up and turn it manually. How much fun is that for a toddler? She has never lasted more than 5 minutes on this toy and we all used to ride ours all day long. There seems to be a disturbing trend with Radio Flyer trying to "nerf" the turning radius of all their products. I have a Radio Flyer wagon with the same issue. You need a grocery store parking lot to turn it around. They're now "safe" but no longer "fun". I will be looking for another version that actually turns.

Watched this with my teenagers and we were all blown away. The animation is gorgeous, but man, this thing goes to some surprisingly dark places. It doesn't hold back or talk down to the audience. I'd highly recommend it to parents of older kids who can handle intense themes, skip it for the little ones. If you appreciate films that treat viewers with respect, grab this immediately.
Picked this up expecting a standard love story but got way more depth. The suspense angle totally caught me off guard. Nora Roberts delivers every single time, this one exceeded what I hoped for.
Can't believe how well this works for under nine bucks. Does exactly what it claims and feels way more expensive than it is. Totally worth every penny.

We are enjoying this series and are looking forward to Season 2. Love those murder mysteries and very glad to have a new one to look forward to with others being completed with BBC. With this series they are able to adjust and write more of and new ones as well.

I received this pond deicer just in time for the second round of cold weather here in Edmond, OK (12 Feb 2013). I removed my failed K&H Perfect Climate Deluxe 1500-Watt Pond De-Icer, which failed during our first 2012 pond freeze, with this device. So far, so good - the heater did become (briefly) operational last night. Although we had a good snowfall, it did not get cold and stay cold - So, there has been no real hard-freeze test of this unit as of yet. I will update this review after we have had a prolonged hard freeze. I am confident it will perform well. But will it endure?

Great investment to keep the quadcopter fun going. Highly recommend when purchasing the Hubsan X4 H107C and H104L. Each flying session on fresh batteries last about tens minutes. Also recommend multi battery charger.
Way more detailed than I expected. Really breaks down the Pacific theater strategy perfectly.
Great show...just like all the other seasons. Have to wonder how much longevity this show will have since they only shoot 3 episodes per season.

Great Cooler. Cheaper than other high end coolers, with what I consider better features. Extraordinarily robust construction, with secure yet easy to operate latches, and well through through double carrying handle options make this the nicest cooler I've ever owned. We recently took it on a 7 day road trip. It did need to be topped off with fresh ice twice, but we were ALWAYS opening it and it was during late May (warm outside). Easy to drain (built with bottom at a very slight slope to drain---- and the train itself can be connected to a garden hose for remote draining. (well thought through product). This 45 qt size is the largest size I could get that would still fit in the trunk of my Toyota Camry, but it's easier to keep in the back seat. It's also just light enough for my wife and I to easily carry when fully loaded. Anything else would be too large and heavy for us.

I've been using this little guy for about three weeks now and I'm honestly impressed. The thing I care about most with a travel mouse is battery life because I'm constantly forgetting to charge my peripherals, and this one just keeps going. I charged it once when I unboxed it and haven't plugged it in since, even with daily use at work and some evening browsing at home. The size took a day or two to get used to coming from a full-size mouse, but now I actually prefer how light it is. Tracking is smooth on pretty much every surface I've tried including my fabric desk pad and the armrest of my couch. Connection has been rock solid with zero dropouts. For the price this is hard to beat.

Been using this for about a month now and honestly it's a game changer for my sweaty hands. I teach vinyasa flow and finally stopped needing to layer a towel over my mat during intense sessions. The grip just keeps getting better the more you perspire. I'd recommend this to anyone doing hot yoga, pilates, or really any practice where you tend to slip. Worth every penny.
Short story with interesting spirit animal themes. The character growth felt a bit rushed but I liked the cultural elements woven in.
These work perfectly for my daughter's 18-inch doll. Great quality fabric and the colors are even prettier in person.
A great short read with an interesting story line. I will be reading more from Mac in the very near future.

This was a bit big- and didn't shape / pull in as much as I was hoping. I am a larger woman and these do hide some of the bulges when wearing a dress/skirt, but there are definitely much firmer holds out there in different brands. This is great if you're looking for a less expensive, moderate definition, but these are not going to make you magically look 40lbs lighter.
It's ok but doesn't let the nipples show well. They look like missiles.
A great size and shape and it is nice to have the tray option. The yellow is lighter than expected, more of a lemon color, but a nice bright pop of color in the dark basement.

I needed to dress up four windows in my living room but really didn't want to drop hundreds of dollars on custom curtains. Took a chance on these and honestly could not be happier. The fabric has a nice weight to it, they hang beautifully and actually block out a solid amount of light without being labeled as blackout. I ordered the grey and it's a true, soft neutral that goes with everything. My favorite part is how expensive they look. I had my sister over last weekend and she immediately asked where I got them because she assumed they were from a pricier boutique. For the price of two panels, you really can't beat this. Washed and ironed well too, no weird shrinking or wrinkling. Already planning to buy another set for the bedroom.

Looked decent at first but after about three weeks the edges started peeling up on their own. Now there's a crack running across the middle and I haven't even dropped my phone. Expected way better longevity for the price. Going back to my usual brand.

Expected more based on the photos. The fit runs small and the band digs in after a few hours, but the cups themselves give decent shape and the fabric feels decent quality. Not terrible for the price, just not the everyday bra I was hoping for.

Picked this up for a weekend read and ended up finishing it in two sittings. The setting of a cozy New England inn is perfect, and I loved how the two main characters play off each other throughout the whole story. The dynamic between them feels genuine, like watching two friends figure out their rhythm while everything around them goes sideways. The mystery itself kept me guessing without being overly complicated or dark. There's a nice balance of humor and tension that makes the whole thing move along quickly. The food and cooking class details add a fun layer without slowing things down. Really enjoyable from start to finish and I'm hoping this becomes a series because I'd gladly spend more time with these characters.

My granddaughter loves loves loves Annie, she's two and walks around the house singing tommorow! Then says "you sing" she gets us all singing:) so we know after buying the DVD and cd of Annie for Christmas they'll both be worn out by February OR sooner lol

This was a solid read but didn't totally blow me away. The romance between Andre and Devon has some genuinely great moments, and their banter kept me turning pages late at night. That said, the pacing really drags in the middle, I found myself skimming through a few chapters that felt like filler. Devon's character arc bugged me too. She's introduced as this confident, take-charge woman, but then she loses all that fire whenever Andre is around, only to snap back to tough mode when the plot needs it. The French captain thing is a fun touch though, and some of the dialogue is genuinely witty. Overall it's an enjoyable way to spend a weekend, just don't go in expecting anything life-changing. I'd read more from this author.

It works out of the box. I was happy for a while until I pause the music and notice the massive static white noise this device is generating in the background. Then I disconnected the receiver from my speaker. My speaker is quiet again. I was hoping for better and returned it soon.

Ordered this for my granddaughter's birthday and it arrived two days earlier than expected. The packaging was really solid, no dented corners or bent spine, which is a relief because I've had bad luck with book mailers lately. They wrapped it in bubble wrap inside a sturdy cardboard sleeve, so it survived the trip perfectly. Opening it up, the cover art is even nicer in person. The pages feel thick and durable, not flimsy like some reprints I've seen. Flipped through a few chapters before gift wrapping it and the illustrations are crisp and vibrant. Honestly the print quality exceeded what I paid for it. My granddaughter is going to love this one. Already planning to grab another copy for my other granddaughter's shelf. Can't go wrong here.

Honestly expected something flimsy for the price but this thing is built like a tank. Fit my Wrangler perfectly and holds up great on rough trails. Way more solid than the cheaper ones I've tried before. Super impressed with the overall quality and finish.

I bought this for an off-white skater skirt my daughter bought that was very thin once we saw it in the full light. This provided the needed coverage to allow her to wear it. The material is nice and has held up for several washings now.
Got two of these for layering on camping trips and they're worth every penny. Wicks sweat well, fits true to size, and holds up after multiple washes. Can't beat the price.
DON'T BEND THEM OR THEY WILL SPLIT. THEY NEED TO BE MORE RESILIENT.

It's a great book! Some scenes took my mind a bit to wrap around, but it was enjoyable. A torture scene, no, the focus of the torture scene, I could've done without, but I realize that's my own issue, not how's it written. I look forward to the next book in the series!
I have improved on my freestyle technique and power greatly thanks to this paddles. They fit perfectly well and look nice

Box arrived pretty banged up and the plastic casing on the receiver had a crack right along the side. Looked like someone just tossed it in a bubble mailer with zero protection. I hooked it up anyway and the sound quality is tinny and cheap sounding. The button feels flimsy like it'll snap after a few months. Honestly expected better for the price. It does ring when pressed so I guess it works, but barely worth keeping.

Couldn't put this one down. Rollins delivers again with a fast-paced adventure that kept me hooked from start to finish. The historical mysteries blended perfectly with the thrills. Fantastic read overall.

Looks and Works Well. I've installed two of these devices now and I like them a lot. My favorites are the Rev-a-Shelf stainless steel models because they're stronger and clean up nicely. Installation can be tricky but take your time and you'll get it right on the first try. I've seen "pro" cabinet installers take two or three tries (with two or three sets of holes) so measure twice, drill once.

Got this for my two older cats who mostly ignore every scratching post I bring home. Surprisingly, they took to it right away. The angled design lets them really stretch out, which seems to matter more now that they're getting stiff. It's held up well after a month of daily use too, no shredding or falling apart. I'd definitely recommend this to anyone with senior cats or really any cat that needs encouragement to scratch something other than furniture. Worth every penny.

There are many books on my shelves - real and virtual - that I started and never bothered finishing. I gave four stars because I wanted to finish this book, and I look forward to finishing the series. Its a darker book than I expected to ever enjoy reading, and I can't say that enjoyment is the right word. But I did find the tale riveting, and I want to know what Jorg does next. I guess that says as much as anything could that Lawrence did a great job.

Got two of these for my kitchen island and honestly couldn't be happier for what I paid. Similar spoon rests at the home goods stores near me run double or triple this price and honestly aren't any nicer. The base catches all the sauce drips so my countertops stay clean during big cooking sessions, and the little stand keeps my wooden spoons upright instead of sliding everywhere. Ceramic feels solid, not cheap or flimsy. I've run them through the dishwasher probably ten times now and no chips or fading. The simple white matches my everyday dishes perfectly. For under fifteen bucks each these are an absolute steal. Grabbed a pair for my mom too after she kept eyeing mine during Thanksgiving prep.

This book has few redeeming qualities, and so needs more competition fast to get it out of circulation forever. The information is outdated, the companion website is useless, and the text colors are mustard and navy blue, which make it very difficult to read. Because I also had an uncreative instructor that completely relied on this horrific textbook for the course that used it, I'm an thoroughly disappointed. There is definitely a market for competition here.
A good product. Not long lasting with our pup but she loves them. Attractive price.

Ordered this on a Monday and it showed up Tuesday morning with Prime, which was a nice surprise. Box arrived in perfect condition with no dents or crushed corners. Everything was packed really securely inside with molded foam. Pulled it out and was honestly surprised by how solid it feels. The stainless steel inner pot is heavier than I expected and the lid has a nice, firm seal. Control panel is intuitive enough that I didn't need to crack open the manual right away. Did a quick water test to make sure everything sealed properly and it came right up to pressure with zero issues. Already planning chili for tomorrow night. First impressions are fantastic and honestly wish I'd bought one sooner.

Ordered this on a Monday and it was at my door by Wednesday afternoon. Box arrived in perfect condition, no dents or crushed corners like I've dealt with from other online retailers. Unboxing was clean and simple, everything tucked neatly in place. Had it plugged in and connected to my wifi within maybe ten minutes. The interface loaded right up and navigation felt intuitive right away. Picture quality blew me away on the first show I watched. Honestly wasn't expecting it to feel this polished straight out of the box.

Honestly didn't expect much for the price, but this little pump exceeded every expectation I had. Needed something to drain my hot tub and thought I'd be waiting forever. Emptied the whole thing in maybe 15 minutes. My old way involved a garden hose siphon which took hours and barely worked. This thing pushes water out fast and with real pressure. Hooked it up to a standard hose and was done before I knew it. Way more powerful than I anticipated. Totally worth every penny.

Got this on sale and honestly it's worth way more than what I paid. The way she writes about family dynamics just rings true on every page. Each character feels like someone you grew up with, and the small-town politics she captures are spot on. I stayed up way too late finishing it. For the price of a coffee you get a genuinely moving story about ordinary people navigating complicated relationships.
Great price for a tool I only needed once. Fit perfectly where my regular ratchet couldn't go and got the job done fast. Definitely worth keeping around.

I really love this wrap but lost it all to a certain someone who shall not be named, who put it in the sink and then did a load of dishes under scalding water. Until its tragic melting, my bees wrap held oranges, bell peppers, lemons, apples, leftovers, sandwiches - you name it. I only wish it wasn't so expensive. I would own a lot more. The sandwich bag will be my next investment when I can afford it.

I had mixed feelings about this one. The writing itself is genuinely strong, and the main character's journey stuck with me days after finishing. That said, the relentless heaviness made it a slog at times. Every chapter piles on more trauma and misery without much relief, which made it hard to stay motivated. I kept waiting for a turning point that never really came. The male lead frustrated me to no end, his decisions felt contrived rather than earned. Still, I can't say I regret reading it. The author clearly has talent and knows how to build complex, flawed people. Just go in prepared for a dark, emotionally draining ride. Three stars because it's memorable but not enjoyable.

Honestly didn't expect much for the price but this puzzle feeder completely surprised me. My orange tab is usually lazy and loses interest fast, but he actually works for his kibble now instead of whining at the bowl. The pieces slide smoothly and nothing has gotten stuck yet. Way sturdier than the flimsy ones I've tried before. Hand washes easily too. Genuinely exceeded what I thought I was getting.

These patches do exactly what they're supposed to and fit perfectly in my chamber. At first I thought they were a bit pricey, but honestly the time saved makes them worth every penny. Will be ordering more soon.
Excellent product for recovering scratched CD's. Combine it with The Skip DR cleaning kit and you will be good to go!
Great performances, especially the lead. Pacing dragged in the middle act, but the science scenes were fascinating.

These are a smaller sized, hard bone. The dog likes them. I like that they have a sort ingredient list and that the company does extensive testing on all of their products. You can even go to their website, enter your product batch numbers and see the results of all the testing.

I rented this thinking it would be an intense drama, but the soundtrack is honestly what ruined it for me. That repetitive, screeching score playing over every single scene made it physically uncomfortable to sit through. I actually had to mute parts because it was giving me a headache. The cinematography is way too dark and shaky on top of that, so you can barely tell what's happening half the time. Combined with a story that goes absolutely nowhere meaningful, this was a complete waste of money. Skip it.

It's great as a mobile projector for presentations and such. The little tripod is a very nice touch and allows for nearly infinite angle adjustments. That said, don't fool yourself into trying to use it as a tiny home theater projector like I did, though. The native resolution is a disappointing 480p, and the digital upscaling looks very grainy, at least with a PS4 driving it. Also, the focus wheel is easily moved, even by vibrations from being mounted on the same wall as the speakers, so it loses focus pretty easily. All in all, I'll probably be getting another projector for the media room and using this one for presentations and such when I'm on the road.

I picked this up on a whim and couldn't put it down. The story pulls you right into the Canadian wilderness alongside Katherine and her mountie husband. What makes it special is how real everything feels, the harsh winters, the friendships, the heartbreak, and the quiet moments that stick with you long after finishing. It's one of those rare books I'll definitely revisit. Already lent my copy to my mom and she loved it too.

We've had this for over a year and it's still holding up great. My 4-year-old is not gentle with things, but the felt hasn't pilled or torn. Makes card games so much easier for little kids. Definitely recommend.
It fits good and I love the colors!. The size appears to be accurate to me, may be just a touch big, but I like it that way.

So far (had it for almost a week) very please with this case. It arrived in a very timely manner, and the keyboard charges and pairs with the iPad Mini as advertised. The sleep/awake iPad function works ok so far, though it needs to be lined up properly to work, and this happens without too much trouble. The materials and workmanship are good. For the price, I would say you get what you paid for and would meet most people's expectations.

Picked these up on a whim and honestly didn't expect much for the price. I've been using them out on the patio for a few weeks now and they've held up great. Sound is surprisingly clear with decent bass for their size. They pair fast every time and the battery lasts me through a full afternoon of listening. Are they going to replace a high-end system? No. But for budget speakers, I'm really impressed. Great bang for your buck.
Really pretty and comfortable. I love it when metals are mixed like this and the elastic makes it adjustable without having to fiddle with a clasp. VERY happy with mine!

Bought two more of the Stamina 1300 exercise bikes for aging family members. Great, easy to use, bike that has features you can explain even to those that are not computer literate. If you want a fancy bike, this isn't it.
my cats stay off my furniture and stay off for quite some time when i put the Ssscat on. I just wish it would last longer.
Highly recommend for curvy girls, the stretch is amazing and it hugged all the right places.
something like %400 cheaper than a store, including shipping. they fit right in to my 5mW laser and so far working perfectly.

Picked this up since my balcony is tiny and I wanted to grow some herbs and veggies. The methods described are pretty practical and I liked how the author breaks down container spacing and vertical setups. Already started a small lettuce patch based on the suggestions. Knocking off one star because some of the plant recommendations felt a bit repetitive across chapters and I wished there were more photos. Still, solid guide if you're working with limited outdoor space and don't know where to begin.

I love Gentle Leaders- well worth money to keep your dog from pulling you down the street. To personalize ours, I sew bows on the nose area- not blocking dog's vision, but off to side- so everyone knows she is not only friendly, but a female as our medium sized dog is a lab/beagle mix- if you never used one, get it and be sure to READ INSTRUCTIONS on how to adjust and use!
Great! Every movie is fantastic! Tom Selleck is perfect in these movies.

UPDATE: I believe this vacuum is now going on 4 years old and still works like a champ. Had to replace the filter twice in that timeframe, but it shows no signs of slowing down. Probably go on for another 4 years. I'll keep it short: No more bags. I have only had to buy a replacement filter once since buying it over 2 years ago. We did a side by side comparison of another upright vacuum, and this pulled more dirt and more pet hair out of the carpet than the other one. Not by a little bit, but by a huge amount. I vacuumed the house before sending the other vacuum back. Then immediately vacuumed the house again: it looked as though I never vacuumed it with the other vacuum; the lint bucket was full. I hope this lasts 15 more years, because I never want to use another vacuum. Oh, I did have to go out and buy a bristle brush attachment.

As stated in another review for an electronics-dimming product I've purchased on Amazon, these are quite useful though a bit overpriced in my opinion. I had considered buying a roll of automobile tint as it's cheaper but decided against it due to the worry it wouldn't cling as well as these and some tints have adhesive on them which would be a no-go for the intended purpose of using on electronics devices. The item works well and the cling is good and fairly stable. It won't cling to everything though so keep that in mind and instead use a small piece of electrical or duct tape if you need to. I use this mainly for some electronics devices connected to my bedroom TV but also for my large blue LED alarm clock and it works great. I have only had to readjust it once or twice in 6 months.

I apologize for my preconceived notions! I honestly thought that most of the recipes would be with sausage. Needless to say, I was wrong and quite pleasantly surprised. The recipes are varied, with breakfasts, lunch and main dishes. They sound delicious with easily obtainable ingredients. There's nothing worse than having to move heaven and earth in trying to source ingredients and then going to a supermarket and nobody knows what the hell u are talking about. The pictures were clear and inspiring. The directions were easy to follow. Overall a great cookbook!!
Great birthday present for my 4-year-old niece. Good size. Great Price. Shipped fast. Perfect condition.

My little girl saw my brother playing with Legos and she is only 2 years old so we got her this and she loves it. They are nice and made well and they stick together pretty well. I don't think she has played with any of her other toys likes she plays with these. They are perfect for a little girl learning to build. Great toy and we'll made.

Been using this coil cord daily in the garage for about four months now. The terminals are solid and the jacket hasn't cracked or frayed yet. Springs back fine. Just wish it extended a bit more without so much resistance.
Bought this for my daughter's birthday and the spinning rainbow feature is awesome. All the ponies light up beautifully in the dark. Even my son wants to play along. Totally worth every penny.
Way better than I expected. Every single one tested strong right out of the package.
Can't beat these for the cost. Sound is super clear and the build feels solid. Works great for my home studio setup.
my 3 yr old loves giving her baby a bath in this adorable doll tub
Arrived two days early in sturdy packaging. Nice aluminum shell, feels well-built. Plugged in my spare SSD and it worked instantly. Great little enclosure for the price.

After about three weeks of reading on my Kindle, the file started glitching and skipping chapters. Had to delete and redownload twice. For a paid book, that's pretty frustrating. Expected way better quality control than this.

Ugh, what to say? You gutted me. The begging was a little Rocky for me, however when I started reading my dad went into icu. It quickly picked up and sucked me in just when the carpet was pulled out from under my feet.awesome read and can't wait for more. Thankfully I have time to heal lol.

I picked this up expecting a pretty standard quirky mystery, but the depth of the family mythology honestly blew me away. The author has built an entire secret society with rules that go back millennia, and it never feels forced or overdone. What surprised me most was how funny it is, I laughed out loud more than once, which I totally didn't see coming from a book about contract killers. The naming convention alone is worth the price of admission. My only complaint is that now I need to go grab the rest of the series immediately because the ending left me wanting so much more. If you're on the fence, just get it. Way better than I anticipated and easily one of my favorite reads this year.

Arrived rattling around in a plain bubble mailer with zero protection. The plastic housing was already cracked and one of the prongs was bent. Flimsy isn't even the word for it. I didn't bother trying to plug it in. Going straight in the trash.
Unfortunately all the bandages are way too small to treat a moderate size wound. I had to go to pharmacy to buy something bigger anyway.
LOVE IT, LOVE IT!! Works great and to be able to listen to music with a beanie on in the winter is GREAT! Love the color too!

Laurel Hawthorne is a woman with secrets, which she believed she'd left behind when she married David and moved into a posh gated community. Until, that is, the night that their daughter's friend drowned in the Hawthornes' pool, a terrible event that put a huge crack in Laurel's carefully maintained facade. The Girl Who Stopped Swimming is a riveting, Southern gothic, family drama, driven by the skeletons in Laurel's closet, her fear for her own daughter, and her love-hate relationship with her glamorous older sister, Thalia. Did someone cause Mollie's drowning? Was it an accident or a murder? What does daughter Shelby know that she isn't telling? Does David have a mistress? Do ghosts really appear to Laurel? And, underlying all these foreboding questions is an even darker one, about a death that occurred during her childhood. The Girl Who Stopped Swimming is a real page turner, from the first to the final chapters.

I really looked forward to getting these having read the reviews but so far have found them awkward in the following ways: 1. The fenders tend to warp easily meaning that if they follow the cross-section curvature at the brake end, they will be way off center at the mud flap end. You can do a limited amount of adjusting but so far I haven't managed to get it quite right 2. Creep - be careful and don't try to get them too close to the tire - no closer than 3/8ths of an inch, otherwise with vibration the forward end of the blade will creep down towards the tire and several times I have caught it between the tire and the brakes. I would recommend keeping the blade about 3/4 of an inch from the rear brake. 3. I use these only occasionally - in Colorado we don't have much rain, so they are on and off, mainly during the winter - they are easy to install, but I do seem to have to spend a lot of time aligning them properly. Might be easier for someone who leaves them on for longer periods - I don't know. I've had them for a couple of months - will stick with them but my enthusiasm has been dented a bit.
My kids have watched this Spider-Man movie three times already this week. Great picture quality and the whole family enjoyed it.
Got this for five bucks and it was worth every penny. Solid performances and a decent redemption story make for an enjoyable watch.

Picked this up for a beach read and it definitely passed the time. What I really enjoyed was how the author wrote the neighborhood dynamics, the passive-aggressive backyard BBQ scenes felt spot-on and genuinely funny. That said, the story kind of stalled in the second half and I stopped caring about what happened to anyone. The dialogue carried it for me, but the plot needed more.

The tp-link classic remote switch that works with Alexa. They work fine once configured. Only thing I don't like is to configure them, you have to run their app on iOS or Android. None exists for Windows so if you don't have one of these smart phones, stay away

Put me down as another person receiving a defective unit. First, the machine never cooled past -7F on "pre-cool". Second, on the first and second batches (I didn't make any more), after the "add mix-ins" alert and mix-ins are added (not many, and not large pieces), the machine sounds like a motorcycle revving in the kitchen non-stop and the paddle stops turning. The ice cream was alternating between "frozen yogurt" and "gelato" on the display, actually reversing back down a couple of times. I'm trying one more time before buying a different brand.

For the money, this impact wrench is hard to beat. Pulled all four corners off my truck for a brake job in minutes. Plenty of torque for lug nuts and suspension parts. Runs on my small compressor without issues. Great budget option.

Honestly didn't expect much for the price but these blew me away. The brightness is way better than the cheap ones I've tried before. Hung them along my backyard fence and they've held up through rain with zero issues. Way sturdier than I anticipated.

I recognize that the main reason most people buy this is for sun protection. I purchased it hoping it would keep my shoulders from freezing when swimming laps outdoors when the temperature is in the fifties and sixties. It does help some. There is a bit more drag when swimming but the design of the short does enable you to stroke without feeling impedance. Considered getting a heavier weight shirt but am glad I went with this lightweight model. I used the size chart and purchased XS. I am 5'3", 108 pounds and 32 A (like, barely). This fit just as I had hoped -- tight but not restricting. No problem putting it on but a bit more challenging taking it off when wet. All in all, a good purchase. Hope to get a few more outdoor swims in this month.
Bought this on a friend's recommendation and couldn't put it down. I've already lent my copy to two coworkers. Perfect for anyone who loves character-driven fiction with real emotional depth.

I've been using this Paula Deen steamer basket for about a month now, mostly for vegetables and dumplings. It does the job well and sits nicely inside my standard stockpot without wobbling around. The construction feels solid enough for everyday cooking, though I wouldn't call it heavy duty. Water drains through the holes properly and my broccoli comes out crisp every time. The handle is a little short for my liking, so reaching into a deep pot can be awkward when things get hot. I also wish the sides were a bit taller for larger batches. Cleanup is easy since it goes right in the dishwasher without any issues. For the price point, it's a decent purchase that gets the job done.
For the price this is a great thermometer. Really like that it folds up so it stores better.
cute as can be - our little dogs loved it

I first picked this up back in the nineties and it completely blew me away. Finding a decent copy all these years later felt like tracking down an old friend. The world-building still holds up incredibly well, Lawrence has this way of describing scenes that just pulls you in without bogging you down. What really struck me on this reread is how well the pacing works. Nothing drags, and the characters actually feel like real people making real choices instead of just props moving the plot forward. My teenager grabbed it off my nightstand and tore through it in a weekend, which says a lot. If you grew up with this one, treat yourself to a fresh copy. If you've never read it, you're in for something special.

I figured this would be another throwaway made-for-TV movie, but it's actually a fun ride. Brendan Fraser does his usual likable action-comedian thing, which honestly saves the whole production. The special effects are hit or miss. Some of the underground creatures look pretty decent, while others had me laughing out loud at how bad they were. Josh Hutcherson plays the typical teenager without being too annoying. The 3D gimmick stuff is obvious and distracting if you're not watching it in 3D, so keep that in mind. Still, the pacing moves along nicely and the set pieces are entertaining enough that I didn't feel like I wasted an evening. Better than I expected going in, not as good as it could've been with a bigger budget.

This towel is great! We took it with us during a long trip and they performed as claimed. I loved that they are not bulky st all and dry really quick. These are my go-to towels when I go to the beach now.

I bought this book about six months ago and have reread it three times since. That's saying something because I'm brutal on my Kindle books, highlighting, bookmarking, flipping back and forth to check details. The story still holds up after multiple reads, which is rare for me. Coop and Lacey's connection feels genuine every single time, not forced or rushed. What impressed me most is how the emotional beats land differently on each reread. You catch things you missed the first go-around. The pacing doesn't drag even when you already know what's coming. I've loaned it to my sister and a coworker, and both came back asking for more from this author. Six months later and it's still sitting in my favorites folder. Solid read that stays with you.
Very nice, good quality. It is very easy to install also.

These lights add a nice warm accent to my bedroom. I wrapped them around the canopy posts of my bed and it looks very inviting and calming. They are bright enough but not so bright that it is hard to look at. They don't seem to heat up too hot although I don't check that very often since they are up so high. They just add a lovely feature to my room that lights it with a warm tone that seems to make everything else in my room look nicer than it really is so I always have them on if guests come over!
The spine started coming apart after about three months of regular use. Content is decent for a basic social studies supplement, but the binding really needs to be sturdier.
It's lifelike, has good details and good quality. My son loves it, and collected dozens of Schleich animal toys. Great educational toys.

Really enjoyed this trilogy overall. The world-building pulled me in and the two main characters have great chemistry. Some sections dragged in the middle book but the finale made it worth sticking around. Not a quick read but rewarding if you put in the time.
Took me about an hour to build solo. Fits perfectly in my living room corner and holds way more than I expected. Really happy with this purchase.
Arrived two days early in perfect condition. Kids went crazy opening it!

Upgraded from an old manual model and what a difference. This thing powers through tough cuts like nothing. Processed twenty pounds of beef in under an hour with zero stalling. Cleanup is quick too. Totally worth every penny.

This movie is not that bad. For an abandoned mine story I think they did pretty well and found myself to be mildly entertained all the way through. Its a typical story, well done. This movie definitely deserves a better rating than most people are giving it.
Perfect for anyone building a multi-monitor setup. Handles my three screens flawlessly.
Dropped this mic twice on concrete and it still works flawlessly.

The Prizzi's are the second best (celluloid) Mafia family ever to come out of New York. The script is a masterwork, the best line deadpanned by Nicholson to John Randolph: "I didn't get married so my wife could go on working (in Brooklynese, it comes out "woiking")." William Hickey, frail, onion skinned, gravel voiced is unforgettable as Don Corrado Prizzi (lovingly called Padrino <Godfather> by Jack's Charlie Partanna). The Oscar won by Angelica Houston was richly deserved in what was her great father's next to last film.

The mystery in this book was as decent and solid as any in the series, but I found it overshadowed by Hamish's bizarre decisions when it came to women. Granted, he's never been particularly successful, but at this point it's been drawn out so far it's becoming painful. In this case a part of it was necessary to the plot, but it still had me cringing to read it. I think this is the part of Hamish's personality that I have difficulty pinning down, despite having read most of the books in the series. Perhaps watching the BBC television series further muddled the waters, because the character there was slightly different than the man I envisioned through reading the books. His attitude towards women and relationships seems slightly off (not in any bad way, but in a 'sometimes old fashioned' and 'sometimes suddenly very modern' way) and it throws the character out of balance. In truth, I'd prefer the series much more if the love life aspect was dropped more by the wayside. There have been some books where it was merely an aside mentioned, and I wish more were that way. Because the series has gone on for so long, and we've gotten to know some of the characters so well, it's in danger of slipping more into a soap opera than a set of neat, tidy little mysteries. Compared to some of the other books in the series, this one was a little lacking. However, compared to a lot of other mystery series, it was still fairly decent. I'd say dedicated fans of the series should definitely read it, but newcomers really should start at the beginning to really understand the dynamics of the relationships. AUDIOBOOK NOTE: The audiobooks in the series have all been spectacularly done, and this is no exception. The accents and voices are top-notch, the reading style is smooth and easy to listen to. Highly recommended as an audiobook.
The directions for assembly were rather vague. It took me longer than necessary to put it together due to the poor directions.

Decent quality for what you pay, but the sizing is pretty limiting. My two-year-old fits okay for now, though she'll outgrow it within months. Fine if you want something budget-friendly that won't break the bank, just don't expect it to last through multiple seasons.

Took this tote on a weekend trip to Vegas and it held up beautifully. Everything I needed fit inside without feeling bulky, makeup bag, change of clothes, iPad, snacks. The water-resistant material wiped clean easily after a minor coffee spill. Zipper feels sturdy and the pockets actually make sense for once. Worth every penny and honestly might grab another color for everyday use. Shipping was fast and the packaging was solid.
Arrived two days early in a sturdy little box. The adapter feels solid and works perfectly with my Nano. Glad I grabbed one before they disappeared.

Bought these for triathlon training and couldn't be happier. The padding is just right for long rides but doesn't feel bulky on the run. Dries super fast after swimming. Fabric feels durable and the sizing was spot on for me. Definitely recommend for the price.

Bought a four-pack for my garage pegboard and ended up ordering another set a week later. These hold my wrenches, screwdrivers, and pliers without sliding an inch. Installation took maybe five minutes total. I'd definitely recommend these to anyone setting up a workshop or even just trying to organize a tool bench in a tight space. Way better than digging through drawers every time you need something.
Way sturdier than I expected and the latch actually works great.
Rebus is back- old fashioned - ubdoubtably but always gets results just before the last page. More of the same please
I enjoy all the Meg Mysteries, but this story highly delightful. I am looking forward to reading some more fun fill stories Thanks for the great entertainment on a cold night!

VERY cute set. Looks just like in the pictures. Came banged up on two of the corners, but this was a gift for a 6 year old with a young brother, so they may do worse damage. She can fit all of her "makeup" and girlie items in the drawer and on top. She loved it! Very easy to assemble.

This series just keeps getting better. Stone is exactly the kind of hero I love, protective without being overbearing. The suspense plot genuinely surprised me, and the chemistry between him and Lily is off the charts. I stayed up way too late finishing it. Shayla Black never disappoints with these characters.
It wasn't my cup of tea. I know most romcoms stink but I gave this one a chance. Bad idea.

This book is likely to make many people very uncomfortable, but I think it's a great alternative version of what is taught in mainstream Christianity. Mary is brought to life as an individual with weaknesses and foibles, and a historical reality that makes a lot more sense than generally (blindly) accepted.

broken notes. by Ann Marie frohoff. *****5***** life just kept throwing tomatoes at us. "for the first time since laying eyes on her as a high school freshman, I felt as if I did not want to fight for our love anymore. that thought made me sink deeper into despair, because I loved her so much." ann marie is literally trying to kill me with these books! they have made my heart pound, bite my nails, and cry big fat ugly tears. aly finally gets to spend the week in London with Jake and she can tell he has changed. now she is back home finally getting ready to graduate and she will officially begin her life with Jake in New York. but aly has reservations about Jake and school. Jake wants her to live with him and go to nyu, but she has schools at home in California that offer her a free ride for four years. aly decides to take the plunge and move to new york with Jake. all is well for a couple of months until tragedy strikes and sends Jake spiralling down. his best friend and band mate dump has fallen ill and ends up dying after getting pneumonia. aly decides to go back home and go to school while she trys to put her life back together. this makes Jake more depressed and starts taking pills and drinking again. during his grief he also makes some life altering mistakes he can never take back which results in aly finally giving up on them. real life is not beautiful all the time, aly and Jake used to believe love was all they needed, until they stopped believing. life is unfair and for this couple l ok 've will throw more at them. sometimes life gets in the way of a happily ever after.

Bought this on a whim and ended up finishing it in two sittings. The pacing really worked for me and the characters felt authentic. I'd definitely recommend this to anyone who enjoys twisty thrillers with a slow burn build up. My sister is reading it next and I already know she'll love it. Great find for the price.

Thought these would be flimsy like the cheap army men from the dollar store, but they're surprisingly detailed and sturdy. My kids have been battling with them nonstop for weeks and not a single one has broken yet.

Honestly didn't think these would do much beyond giving my pup a treat, but the difference is noticeable. My vet commented on less tartar at his last checkup, which shocked me. They smell a little funky to us humans, but my golden goes absolutely bananas for them every morning. A bit pricey compared to basic dental sticks, but they actually work so I'll keep buying.
AS WITH MASK OF COMMAND PARTS TOO COMPREHENSIVE, SOME OPINIONS STRAINED. OVERALL GOOD ACCOUNT.

Put this up over my desk lamp and what a difference. The harsh glare is completely gone but I still get plenty of light for paperwork. Took maybe two minutes to assemble. Looks clean and professional too. Gonna grab a second one for the lamp by my reading chair.

I bought this for my 12-year-old who tends to spend too much time on screens, and honestly it grabbed him in a way I didn't expect. He read the whole thing over a weekend and actually came to me wanting to talk about the ending. That alone makes it worth every penny. The language is simple enough for middle schoolers but the themes hit hard no matter your age. I found myself tearing up reading it myself after he finished. If you're a parent trying to pull your kid into something meaningful, or a teacher looking for a class read that actually sparks real conversation, this is the one. Also makes a lovely gift for anyone going through a rough patch.
Highly recommend this brush for anyone with a stinky, shedding pup. Mine smells amazing now and her coat looks so shiny. Grooming is way easier too!
Great value for the price. Super simple to install and the remote lets you switch between tons of color modes. Brighter than I expected. Definitely recommend.
Arrived quickly and well-protected in sturdy packaging. Smaller than I expected but surprisingly sturdy. Great for quick touch-ups without dragging out the full board.
Smaller than I expected but decent for the price.

This is a fantastic keyboard and mouse combo. I love the fact that there is an indicator on the keyboard that tells you when you need to replace the battery. Typing on the keyboard is a little tricky and takes some getting use to, but still this is a great product. I could not have wish for anything more. Edit: So far since purchased only the letter "M" key has started to fade. Still a great buy!
My 8 pound Rat Terrier Loves these. They are made of had plastic so they will last for quite a while.
Watched this with my kids and we all loved it. The action sequences are incredible and the emotional moments really land. Great addition to our Marvel collection.
Perfect for backing up phone photos. Grabbed a couple for my parents' tablets.

You needn't be an historian, geologist or even American to be captivated by this insightful and entertaining examination of those movers and shakers of American history (well-known and unknown) who helped to guide it...and the world...through extraordinary accomplishments.
Beautiful dragon, bought him for me but my daughter stole him so guess I need to get another, lol!!

I purchased this belt because the XL was too small. Well, of course this belt is too big. I will be adding another hole to make it fit me. I wish there were a size in between the two, otherwise it's a good purchase.

Bought this for my mom after her stroke and it's been a game changer for her at dinner. She can cut everything on her plate independently now without switching to a knife. Honestly anyone dealing with grip issues or one-handed limitations needs this. Well made and actually comfortable to hold.
Picture quality way better than I expected for the price.
Not worth the money. Too short for what they charge and the typos got really distracting by chapter three.

This is a well designed case. I love the way it feels and will protect the iPad air. It just seems sturdier than most other cases and will not slip off a table if bumped by a grand kid (extra bonus for me). I will be shopping this brand for other things, I think.
Arrived two days early in secure packaging. Opened it up and was genuinely surprised by the solid build quality. Glides smoothly across my phone screen right away.

Got this poncho-style jacket about eight months ago and it's held up way better than I expected. I'm normally a women's small in US brands and ordered a large based on the size chart, which worked out perfectly. The fabric is thicker than most capes I've tried and hasn't pilled or lost its shape after dozens of washes. I wear it to walk the dog every morning in 40-degree weather and it still looks new. No loose threads, the buttons are firmly attached, and the collar keeps its structure. My only complaint is the arm openings are a bit snug if you're wearing a thick sweater underneath. Definitely runs small compared to American sizing so size up at least one or two. Solid quality for the price.

The world depicted by this book (and what I believe will be expounded on in the next two sequels) follows a few principles of communism - equality, submissiveness, trust, and longevity of life. Thus, the main conflict is on the issue of "freedom of choice". Being "Matched" infers that a pairing decision was made by someone else (an authority) and is similar to an arranged marriage. The story focuses on how 3 teens cope with this reality as they "come of age". The book is written from a first person perspective. It is very well written and edited. The pacing is slower than other dystopian contemporaries, but the flipside of this is a richer narrative of the main character's thoughts, logic, emotion and confusion (I wouldn't quite define it as angst). Because life is often a battle between money and principle, love and infatuation, organization and creativity, and democracy and autocracy, I think this book is relevant to most people. It all depends on whether you have the time to spend thinking about these thoughts and options.

These are comfortable, sound good, and the build quality is great. A battery is included (and the case has a place for an extra, which is nice - there are several upgrades over the old model: The cord is detachable if damaged, and you can continue to use the headphones (with the same sound quality and volume, as far as I can tell) when the batter runs out on the noise-cancelling function. Worth the price for the quality.

I had high hopes for this stone based on all the glowing reviews, but honestly it hasn't lived up to the hype at all. The first couple of pizzas came out okay with a decently crispy bottom, but nothing mind-blowing compared to my old stone that cost half the price. Then on maybe the fifth use, I pulled it out of the oven using both hands and heard this awful crack. Split right down the middle and my pizza slid straight onto the oven door. What a mess. I always let it cool down gradually too, so thermal shock wasn't even the issue. For the money they're charging, I expected something way more durable. My cheap one from Target lasted me eight years, this barely made it a month.
Fun holiday watch with great ensemble cast. Some storylines landed better than others but overall enjoyable.
Read these constantly for months and the bindings still hold up perfectly.
Perfect for anyone with a farmhouse bedroom. So soft!
Grabbed these on a whim and so glad I did. Four hangers for under ten bucks is a steal. Pants stay flat and crease-free in the closet. Solid build quality too. Might order another set soon.

Been using this for about six weeks now. The container itself held up fine, no cracks or leaks. As for the actual results, my pup's breath is slightly better but definitely not gone. It's decent enough to keep buying but don't expect miracles.
Love how lightweight this comforter is, keeps me warm without feeling heavy. Great quality for the price.
Thought it'd be overrated but the performances genuinely blew me away. Glad I bought it.

My dog (100lb mastiff, injured cruciate) couldn't walk well in the brace. She swayed and almost fell. The brace was a tid bit too long but the strap over the hips was good idea to keep it from slipping. Going to exchange for a different size, I hope it works better.
Perfect fit for my setup. Highly recommend to anyone with a black DSLR looking for clean protection.

I really wanted to love this bathrobe since the material feels decently soft at first touch, but after wearing it for about three weeks I'm pretty underwhelmed. The fit is just weird, the shoulders are super boxy but the sleeves run short, so it feels awkward to actually lounge in. The belt loops are placed too high on the torso, which means the whole thing bunches up weirdly around my waist. I threw it in the wash on cold like the tag suggested and the seams along the pockets already started fraying. For the price I honestly expected better construction. It's not the worst robe I've owned but I wouldn't buy it again or recommend it to anyone. Pretty disappointing overall and definitely not worth what they're charging.
Perfect Christmas present for a family that wanted the movie, so the could see 2.

In the age of digital revolution that metamorphosed photography for ever there is little reason to buy an analog product. A product with a large size and cumbersome function. rather than take hundreds of photos before you think of changing your memory card you are limited to 10 small expensive prints. So why did I buy it. And how come did I give it five stars. Will here are the reasons; 1. I had realistic expectations for what I was getting into. 2. I love photography in all it's shapes and forms. 3. Again I had realistic expectation. 4. I'm into fun stuff 5. I'm sentimental for the old days. look at reasons 1 and 3 again. some people buy things with inflated expectations and then get disappointed with what they have. Honestly the photo quality was way way much better than I expected. The colors are vivid and vibrant and the focus is really sharp. The camera has for simple different settings for light conditions and an automatic flash which did a really good job for foreground lighting. The photos are small but that was a compromise I'm willing to make. They are expensive $1 per print, to me that makes them more precious. In this day of digital we are so used to click click click with our digital cameras and our cellphones. We take thousands of photos and print literally zeros. The photos we take are free so we don't think twice of click click click. With this Polaroid and $1 per print price tag I enjoyed the click and the "Shake it like a Polaroid picture" and the sharing with others. It is really really fun. But I have no illusions. And I want you to have none. This is a novelty / speciality item. It will not replace my DSLR or my point and shooter it will compliment them and thus I'm really happy with my purchase and if you buy this camera with a realistic expectations and if you are a sentimental guy/gal you will enjoy it like I am.
This DOCSIS 3.0 high-speed modem is a great match for our 25Mbps cable and gigabit network.

Didn't expect much when I grabbed this on a whim, figured it'd be another predictable travel-inspired read. Man, was I wrong. The storytelling grabs you from chapter one and doesn't let go. I actually stayed up way too late two nights in a row because I couldn't stop turning pages. The cultural details feel incredibly authentic without being heavy-handed. Way beyond what I paid for.

I had my doubts about this product but when I started using it I realized there are a lot of possibilities to use this. It's stainless steel so it will match any kitchen decor. It's very attractive so it can be displayed on the table or put in the cabinet with the spices. It's pretty lean so it won't take up much space but it held more oil than I expected. I put Olive Oil in it (not the person) and it won't great. It was easy to spray after one or two priming pumps. The oil comes out in a line, I wish there was a way to make it have more of a spray but the oil does come out very easily. So I used the olive oil as a pot coating like a cooking spray. I could also see this being nice for salad dressing oils because it will limit the amount of dressing put on the salad. I received this product complimentary in exchange for my honest opinion but the opinion expressed is all my own

I read some negative remarks on the pack but thought it was a good choice considering it had over 200 positive remarks. For $48.00 I couldn't have found a better bag. It has plenty of room and the quality seems to be pretty tough. It's not a 250.00 tamrac but for the money I would say it's a better buy.
Eliminates big bulky dressers, thank goodness. I was able to trash my big ugly wooden dresser, at last. Great stuff, high quality, have owned mine for almost a year now.
These look amazing!! But know that cutting them can be difficult. But the appearance is outstanding and they STICK!! Have had up over a year & still look great. Get lots of compliments.

Got this for my Maine Coon and honestly can't believe the price point for the quality. Other cat harnesses I've looked at were double this and felt flimsy. This one is sturdy, the fit is spot on, and she actually tolerates wearing it. Great bang for your buck if your cat likes going outside.

I had high hopes for this one given the cast, but man was this a slog. Expected something sharp and clever based on the description, but instead it's mostly people talking in circles at the same restaurant table for what feels like forever. Jesse Plemons does what he can with the material, and Jeffrey Tambor is reliable as always, but nobody can save dialogue this self-indulgent. The big ending everyone talks about? Total letdown. I kept waiting for it to click and it just never did. Disappointing.

I saw this complaint all over the internet from 2014 up to 2016 and they still haven't fixed the problem. Also called WD and I couldn't get a straight answer why they sell the 2TR drive but yet its not advertised on there website when you try to purchase one direct from the WD website (They only show 1,4,5,6 TR versions at checkout) So I called the technical sales line and they told me to call the technical sales line (yes you read that correctly) I'm so confused by this whole ordeal. Good quality. Easy to apply n sticks really well to the wall. Tip: Clean wall with alcohol prior to apply n let it dry before applying it

Lost my original Minn Kota fob overboard last season and finally pulled the trigger on this replacement. Ordered it on a Monday and it was at my door by Wednesday afternoon, nicely packed in a sturdy box with plenty of padding so it wasn't rattling around. Feels identical to the factory remote that came with my Terrova. Solid little unit, good weight to it, buttons have a nice tactile click. Paired it up in about ten seconds, no fuss at all. Range seems just as good as the original. Comes with the lanyard and battery already installed, which was a pleasant surprise. Honestly can't tell any difference between this and the one that shipped with the motor brand new. Great value compared to buying direct from the dealer.
Worth every penny. The writing pulls you in and doesn't let go.

Grabbed these for my morning gym sessions and they've been awesome. The cushioning holds up well on the treadmill and during lunges. I'd definitely recommend them to anyone doing mixed workouts, my lifting buddy tried mine on and immediately ordered his own pair. Fit runs true to size with just enough room in the toe box. Can't beat the value.
Worth every penny. Season two looks way better than the first, and that gag reel alone makes this set a must-buy.

Great net that is as advertised 'soft'. It is great for transferring my Betta from one tank to the other for cleaning. I have an 8 quart tank and could have gotten the next size down for this tank but the net fits into the top just fine. Great value and superb craftsmanship have me recommending this SoftNet by Tetra.

I honestly expected these to feel a little sturdier based on the photos. The leather is almost too soft, like, slipper-soft, which feels great on foot but makes me wonder how they'll hold up long-term. That said, they're surprisingly comfortable right out of the box with zero break-in time needed. I wore them to the office for a full day and had no issues at all. The sole is thinner than I anticipated, so I wouldn't reach for these on rainy commutes, but for dry weather and indoor wear, they're solid. Sizing runs maybe a half size large, I usually wear a 10.5 and the 10 fits perfectly with medium socks. Not the everyday beater shoe I originally pictured, but a really nice spring option for the price.

This very enjoyable story takes unusual directions from the start. Our heroine is a young woman of respectable background who has had her life ruined by a wealthy man. She has been banished to a convent. Meanwhile our hero is a man who went to America as a trader but took the losing side in the Civil War and has returned, with only a ship, to take up a family title. He learns that he will relinquish the lands if he does not marry and produce an heir by a certain date, which is only a few months away. This option appears to be impossible until as luck would have it, he meets our heroine who is in an interesting condition. A somewhat creepy item is that the hero's ship carries the remains of his mother whom he wishes to bury on her family land. Why he could not do that without owning the land is unclear. The wish seemed tossed in to make the hero more altruistic and less land-grabbing. We are reminded of a certain character several times by the heroine pointedly asking what can have become of her or a message she carried. This builds to a suspense finale which again is most unusual in period romances. Read, enjoy, be surprised. There is lots of fun historical detail such as how inheritance worked and this has to be explained to the hero for our benefit, he having been in America for twenty years.

Bought these for my kid's ocean-themed birthday party and honestly expected better. The designs themselves are super cute and my son was excited about the sharks and turtles. Problem is the actual print quality is noticeably off. All the colors look slightly misaligned so everything has this weird blurred halo effect around it. Up close the animals just look messy and out of focus. For the price I really thought these would be sharper and more polished. My kid still played with them but kept asking why the fish looked funny. Packaging was fine and shipping was quick but the final product just feels cheaply made. Wouldn't buy again and honestly can't recommend them to anyone looking for decent quality.

Boy shags strainge woman in the woods, and starts to change into a female everytime he gets excited. meets beautiful girl who also turns into a boy, they take turns. Only good thing about this stupid movie is that the boys are cute. Don't waist your time.

Bought this two seasons ago for my kids' soccer games and it's still going strong. No rips, frame hasn't bent at all. Worth every penny.
Perfect for my anxious pup. Highly recommend to fellow dog owners!

We love this movie better than the original Wizard of Oz, not that the original was bad. Just we love this rebooted version more. It is a dip into sci-fi and fantasy, a bit darker direction as well. But it was great! I am certain most younger generation will love this movie.
Bought for fellow teacher who has her classroom decorated in Seahawks fun. (Even her students bought Seahawk shirts to go impress the teacher!

Caveat, I love Dominion. This is more Dominion, so I love it. The new mechanics are fun, and give some good variability to the game. Debt is an interesting mechanic, but it ultimately is just a new and limited form of currency that only applies to 6 of the 29 kingdom cards and 1 each of the events and Landmark cards. Otherwise it is unused throughout the entirety of Dominion except for those 8 cards (so far). It was supposedly conceived as a sequel to Prosperity, but I have to say I would not have clued in on that without doing some reading. There are more Victory cards sure, but the highest costing Kingdom card is 5 coins if you don't consider the few cards that cost 8 debt. If you are the sequel to Prosperity, that introduced 5 coin Platinum treasure and 8 coin cost cards, well you see my confusion as to calling this a sequel. It brings back the split upgrade feature of Adventures, but only on one item, the castles, which pretty much only provide victory points. It offers 2 duration cards, SeaSide is still my favorite expansion so yay there. Adventures was a far more extensive expansion, I understand it is bigger by design but this feels underwhelming after that one. This actually feels like an expansion to the Adventures expansion, as we get more events and the locations mechanic feels like an extension of the event mechanic. If you love Dominion, have at, but this is probably down around Hinterlands territory for me in the order of acquisition, ie one of the last expansions someone building a dominion set should worry about buying.
Arrived next day in retail packaging. Fit perfectly and feel super breathable.
Thought it'd be flimsy for the price but it's super sturdy. My lab destroys everything and this has held up perfectly for eight months.
Loved the balance of action and romance! Perfect for paranormal romance fans who want steamy chemistry without sacrificing plot. Couldn't put it down.

Update: July 11, 2014 I hade been giving the Princess Pia (my aging Siamese cat) other products to help ease her arthritic joints, asd could not see any improvement. Then I tried Nutramax Cosequin. But when I tried mixing Nutramax into her food, she wouldn't eat her food. I can't think WHY I keep thinking my (overweight) cat will starve, but I finally overcame my fear and kept putting it into her food - and she finally ATE it! Well, to keep this short, She now gets it twice daily and she eats it every time. She also runs, jumps, climbs, and chases a little "critter" that hangs on a long string from a helium balloon (her current favorite toy). She hadn't been doing any of those things, and had a bad limp. Now she's very active and her limp is barely perceptible. I'm ordering it again.
Excellent. How many more words must I write to be polite? Seriously, if one is satisfied stop with the word requirements!

For the price, these are decent. The material feels thin and the stitching could be better, but they look fine on and held up after one wash. Not something I'd rave about, but fair enough for what I spent.

The silicone seal is super comfortable and doesn't leave those red raccoon rings around my eyes like my old Speedos did. I've got a pretty narrow face and the smallest nose bridge piece fits perfectly. Visibility underwater is excellent too, the lenses give a nice wide peripheral view. My husband tried them last weekend and immediately ordered his own pair. I'd definitely recommend these to anyone who swims regularly, whether you're doing competitive laps or just leisure swimming. They'd also be great for triathletes since the double strap keeps everything secure during flip turns. Honestly for the price point you really can't go wrong.

I had a windows test server that was crashing every day after I upgraded it from 8gb of memory to 12gb. I spent over a week online on various IT support forums trying to resolve it. One day, after running a memory test for 8 hours, the server refused to boot. After letting it sit for 8 hours, it booted just fine. I immediately suspected a heat issue. I purchased these coolers because they were active air movers versus just passive metal coolers. (and the price was right) They've worked perfectly. My server has not crashed once since I installed them!! It runs much faster, and I've not noticed an increase in noise. Prior to installing them, the chips were extremely hot to the touch after a memory test. Today with the Air Coolers, they barely luke warm. They are also extremely easy to install. Once you attach the mounting brackets, they just snap on. I HIGHLY recommend these Airflow Memory coolers.

Well.....what a story! It is so hard to review without saying too much, so I will try. I went into it kind of blind, wondering what it was about as i loved the cover and title and just wanted to read it. Having been through so much so young, Lacey let it all go one night and did something she had never done before...slept with a man, drop dead gorgeous, instant attraction off the charts met in the club Caine. I mean sh^t it was melting how they were almost at it on the dance floor. What a charming wonderful guy Caine was...romantic, caring, singing to her in front of everyone, melted my heart...a perfect gentlemen, opening car doors, a little possessive, but that is because he loves her right?....he has an alcoholic mom and a troubled past and when events in life take a surprising twist....she agrees to marry him, even after a hiccup in their relationship...after visiting the courthouse, he stops opening the car door! Where did the gentleman go? My Heart Sank. That was it..after reading along happily i was suddenly stopped dead......wow! You think you have been got with what is coming next....no, you haven't!! I loved the relationship between Lacey and Becca, i was laughing out loud during their conversations and in the hospital during the quiz i was freaking screaming! (You will know when you read it) From 'The Pussy Doctor'....to 'Aunt Flo visiting', to the Smurf scene, i could barely breathe and this was such light relief after i had been through the drainer living through Lacey's life. I hate to think what Lacey would have done without her friends and family. I wanted Becca's con's list carved into his back with a rusty knife.......i think Becca would have done it. The ending was something else, one thing.....then another, all wrapped up teetering over the edge of a sharp dark cliff edge! Bring on the next enstallment!

I honestly thought this would be a dense, outdated slog that I'd force myself to finish. Instead, I blew through all thousand-plus pages in about a week and a half. The political stuff is definitely dated and parts of it are uncomfortable to read with modern eyes, but the sheer scale of the story hooked me completely. Scarlett is way more complicated and ruthless than any film adaptation lets on, and Rhett's sarcasm hits different on the page. I expected melodrama and got something closer to a survival story. The Civil War backdrop feels enormous but the writing stays totally readable. Way better than I anticipated and honestly one of those books I wish I could read again for the first time.

I've owned many-a steam mops in my day, and while this is a great mop that heats up quickly and offers a bunch of nice features, I wouldn't pay the hundred and forty dollars they're asking for this. There are just too many other, cheaper models out there that get your floors just as clean. Now, if you're someone who sees yourself using the detachable handheld all the time, then this may be the steam mop for you. I THOUGHT I would use it all the time, but in reality I only used it once...the first day I got it. The machine is light and easy to use while you're actually using it, but if you need to set it down, it's prone to tipping over due to it's shape. The tank seems to go through water faster than other mops I've used, but maybe that's just me. Or maybe the steam gets hotter than other mops I've used, so it goes through water quicker? I don't know, but I do know I have to fill the tank three times to get all the hardwood/tile/stone in my house done. Overall, would I recommend this mop? Absolutely. I just don't think it's necessary for the amount of money it costs (unless you really want that detachable steamer). There are plenty of high-quality steam-mops out there for much cheaper.

Had to tighten it twice in the first week and added a drop of threadlock to keep it solid. That said, the weight distribution is spot on and presses chicken breasts uniformly without me hovering over the stove. Cleanup is decent as long as you don't let grease sit in the grooves overnight. Overall pretty happy with it for what I paid.

An awesome film about a lost party who finds themselves on a doomed flight to... where? To a mysterious land which seems to be paradise. But it is a paradise with a purpose... This classic film, made even as the war clouds were gathering in Europe and armies on the march in Asia, is well-acted and an excellent adaptation of the original novel. I even love the lamasery, even though it seems more art deco than Tibetan. To be sure, this film was restored through several techniques, replacing lengths of film removed when it was originally shown, using stills to supplement surviving adio, etc. This technique was used with Metropolis and I think it works at least as well with Lost Horizon.

Ignoring common sense and warnings on the Web, I bought one of these hand held Dyson DC34 vacuums for about 4 times what I'd pay for a high end competitor's model (e.g. Black and Decker LiIon model). When I got it, I was initially very pleased: Good suction, worked very well. The battery life is short (maybe 6-7 minutes on a charge), but I knew that going in, and that's just part of the package. Then, after a number of months, the machine began stopping after a few seconds. It wasn't that the battery wasn't charged: I could use it for a few seconds, have it stop, play games with the unit, get it to run for maybe 20-30 second, have it stop, play more games, and so on to get the full 5-6 minutes out of it. But the unit just failed to run continuously for more than a fraction of a minute. I went online and discovered this is a horribly common problem with this model. I contacted Dyson and had them tell me that the unit was out of its (short) warranty and that I'd need to buy another one. That's not going to happen. Instead, I'll try to warn you all not to waste your money on this machine that, apparently, has design defects. Instead, get a high end (LiION battery) Black & Decker, or similar model for a quarter of the price. It might not be 100% as good as a vacuum, but it's assuredly 90% as good, and in addition, you'll get a much longer lifetime out of it. And when it dies, you can buy another THREE before you're up to the cost of this piece of poorly designed junk. I was burned. You don't have to be.

I have a 10 gallon planted tank with some slow eating fish, so I end up overfeeding them to make sure they all get some sustenance. I had an Eheim 2211 and it worked as hard as it could, but it just wasn't cutting it, and it was an enormous hassle to prime it whenever it needed cleaning - and it needed cleaning constantly, because I was asking it to do too much. I knew I needed a bigger filter and as soon as I realized the Ecco was self-priming, I was sold. Some key differences between the Ecco and the Classic: 1. The priming pump - huge key. I didn't find the plastic handle to be too flimsy, but I was also gentle with it after having read reviews. It primes so quickly and I don't have to worry about swallowing aquarium water! 2. There's only one nozzle on each tube that controls the flow rate, when on the Classic there was two. For me, this is just easier - less connections mean less possibility for leaks, and I only need one nozzle to control the flow rate anyway. 3. It is so much quieter. Maybe it's because I upgraded the size of the filter so much so it's working less, but I'm amazed that a filter so much bigger is so much quieter. It makes NO noise, it's actually incredible. So all in all: powerful, quiet, easy to set up and easy to maintain. I'm so happy with it.
