When the story begins, we are in the mind of Elsie, a smart, funny and sarcastic if a bit average teenager. Elsie and her sister, Julie, (Elizabeth and Jane Bennet) who is one year older than she are have moved from their home in Massachusetts and are going to a new prep school called Coral Tree. There, they meet Chase and Chelsea Baldwin (a.k.a. Charles and Caroline Bingley) and Chase's friend, Derek Edwards (Will Darcy), a handsome, guy who happens to have famous parents. Elsie immediately pegs him as a rich jerk, with girls fawning over him and guys wanting to be him. She also makes friends with Webster Grant, a clever funny guy who, for some reason Derek hates. As the story unfolds, you learn the stories between why Derek is so sheltered, why he hates Webster, and who he really is. This was the best remake of Pride and Prejudice I have ever read, it is true to the story but, still not predictable and leaves you anticipating the characters actions. Lazebnik did not just take Elizabeth and Will, rename them, and stick them in a modern-day high school, she molded and tweaked them, but only slightly, to fit the setting and story. I still love/like Elizabeth, Jane, Darcy, Georgia and Mr. Benton, still am annoyed by Lydia and Mrs. Benton, and still despise Caroline (Does not have Mr. Collins). There is some romance and cussing, but nothing very bad. I really enjoyed this book and loved watching Elsie and Derek grow as characters until the very end. This was so good, I truly encourage you to pick this up and start reading.

I purchased a couple of these devices because I recently got DirecTV and their Genie-Mini does not output optical audio and I have two older receivers that don't accept digital coax input and don't accept audio via the HDMI cable the video comes in on. So, I took a standard 3-prong RCA cable (yellow video and red and white audio) and plugged one end of the yellow RCA cable into digital port of this converter, and the other yellow RCA end into my DirecTV Genie-Mini and then on the other side of this converter, I plugged in my optical audio cord that went to my receiver. Voila! Got back my audio! Glad to not be listening to my DTV via my flatscreen's speakers anymore. Can't comment on how long they last, but they DO work.

These made my dog sick. The other night she was vomiting all night long and had terrible diarrhea. The poor girl was so nauseated that she was dry heaving after completely emptying her stomach. It went on and on for hours. She basically was suffering the same symptoms that humans do when they have a very bad case of food poisoning, complete with chills and shivering, very sore abdominal muscles, weakness and dehydration. It was just awful for her. In the morning, of course, we rushed her to the vet. That was Saturday morning and now it's the wee hours of Monday (I can't sleep) and she's still at the animal hospital having IV fluids, anti-emetics and antibiotics. The vet hopes she can come home later today, but it might not be until tomorrow. The ONLY thing my dogs ate that was new and different in the last several days were these T-Bonz. They each got 2 as a treat. I didn't think the treats had anything to do with it until my other dog had diarrhea and threw-up a bit that morning before we went to the vet. Thankfully he only vomited once and only had a bit of diarrhea and soft stools a couple of times. My pup that got sick had a splenectomy last year, so I'm thinking that whatever was in these treats that made them sick, her body just had a much harder time fighting it off than his. I strongly recommend you NOT give your dog these treats unless you're willing to risk a hefty vet bill, a very sick and completely miserable dog, along with a miserable family, themselves all sick with worry. (Also, just in case it helps anyone or helps add some perspective and clarity: my dog that got very sick is a 20lb schnauzer female, 10 years-old. The dog with a bit stronger stomach is a cocker spaniel-mix male that's 37lbs and he's roughly 10 years-old, give-or-take a couple of years.

Purchased two of these for a recent overnight backpacking trip. Worked really well to compress my "car" camping Coleman mummy sleeping back down to a more manageable size and strap to my pack. Used the second bag to compress the clothes I brought along to save space. Seem fairly well built and did not fray at all when I pulled the straps tight. Zippered pocket on the top is useless.

This is a generous portion of cat food with a lot of higher quality ingredients compared to other types of cat food. Primarily, the first ingredient is actual chicken and it includes taurine. It also does not include grains of any kind and, as anyone knows about cat nutrition, grains are not a part of anything a cat needs in its diet. They are junk food and fillers so the primary selling point of this product is that it provides what your cat needs to be healthy without things which it does not need or that can damage its health. So, I'm very pleased with the ingredients list for this product, though I will note that one pays a premium price for the quality. I can't speak to whether or not the nutrition is as good as the ingredients list indicate it should be, but I can say my cat throws up less after eating this than some other types of food. I can also say that she tends to rapidly lose interest in dry food that I feed her and has held interest in this longer than other types of dry food. I don't know if she's finding it more appetizing (possible, since it has a strong smell and she seems to like stronger smelling food) or if not having digestive issues after eating it makes her favor it. In terms of value, if my cat continues to eat this up over time, I will keep buying it because, though it may be more expensive, if she will actually eat all of it and I don't have to throw out uneaten food, it'll save me money in the long run.

Sweet knife. Fit and finish was spot on. blade was centered and strait in the handle when closed. Opening is smooth and easy. Lock up with liner lock is tight with no play in any direction. Pretty dang sharp out of the box. That being said, the edge is very fine and I rolled about a third of it over in the middle of the blade just dinking around the house with it to get used to it. A quick hone and a few strokes on the strop brought it back easily. I'll update stars and review after I get some time in on it out in the field. It should be noted that this thing is pretty large for an EDC/utility knife. It's heavy for it's size as well, largely due to the solid stainless liners. The blade is broad, but the flat grind mitigates that a bunch. Update - So, I've had this thing a while now and it's been a great blade. I would dock half a star if I could due to the blade having lost it's centering when folded. It now rubs a bit on the liner, though it's not enough to hinder the opening of the knife. I carry it daily and use it daily as well. I feel like I've done anything out of bounds to get the blade to off center like it has. I take care of my gear. That all being said, it's still reliable and a great performer. It holds the edge well and is easy to touch up in the field with my pocket hone. I still recommend this knife and have bought the Rat II and am waiting on a D2 version the the Rat I. Go get one today!

I am a fan of Doreen Owens Malek, her dialogue is witty and she steadily builds the romantic tension between her leads. Her novel, A Marriage of Convenience, is an enjoyable read albeit this is an older novel and there is a sense of the characters being behind the times in many ways. Sharon Phillips returns to her father's ranch in California after his death, she is not happy to be in the Golden state though because she must face her first love, Taylor Braddock. Sharon fell in love with Taylor when she was only eighteen when she visited her father for his wedding to Taylor's mother. Taylor broke her heart and she has not been in contact with him since. Taylor is a Vietnam vet (yes, this is dated) and he has had major difficulties adjusting back into civilian life. He was a POW and he deals with his memories with alcohol and a bad temper. He is about ten years older than Sharon and he finds her sweet and innocent, way too innocent for the likes of him so he sends her on her way making her believe that she is way too young for his jaded tastes. Ten years later the two are forced to confront their feelings because Sharon's dad has bequeathed the ranch to the both of them but they must marry to inherit. They decide to go through with a marriage with plans to annul it fairly quickly but Sharon realizes her feelings for Taylor have not gone away. This novel deals with some big issues like post traumatic stress, loneliness and fear. Taylor is very complex and he is hurting. His transformation from an angry young man to a mature businessman is believable. Sharon is not as interesting as Taylor (how could she be, she's a run-of-the-mill lawyer in Philadelphia) but she is generous and it is her giving heart that makes such a difference in Taylor's life.

I wasn't sure what to expect going in since the plot sounded like it could easily become just another medical drama. But it was better than I anticipated. The author really focused on Katie's emotional recovery rather than just the physical rehab, which gave the story more depth than similar books I've picked up. The chemistry between her and Rob developed at a pace that actually felt real instead of rushed. My main complaint is that Darren's storyline kind of trailed off without a satisfying resolution. I wanted more closure on that end. The writing itself is clean and readable though, and I finished it in two sittings. Not life changing, but definitely held my attention better than most indie romance I've read lately.

The author could have gone into more depth about covert bigotry. THe main character while chastising his son was also guilty of judging based on skin color and religious preference and materialism. The author should be commended for presenting the bigotry on the part of Muslim's and that it cuts both ways. This was a fun, clever book. I was leery of an indie title, but this one was better written/edited than many. This book gives you the perspective of a group of people playing a dungeons-and-dragons-type game, as well as the characters in the game (the NPCs, or non-player-characters). I'll admit as I was reading the book, I at first thought he spent too much time in the characters and I wanted him to "pull back out" to show the people playing the game; but in hindsight, he balanced it just right. He wrote it in such a way that keeps the reader guessing to figure out which characters are truly NPCs and which ones are being "played" by the people in the real world. The characters were all well-developed. The writing style was simple and easy to read, sometimes feeling much like a D&D module, but adding in enough of the feelings and motivations of the characters to make them feel more real. I would recommend this book, especially to those who have ever played D&D.
Wore it daily for about two months and the cross started peeling badly. The soft material scratches super easily and now it looks cheap and cracked. Pretty disappointing.

I picked this up expecting a fairly standard Hollywood memoir, but it's way more intense than I anticipated. Polanski doesn't hold back at all, the stuff about surviving the Krakow ghetto during WWII is genuinely harrowing, and the chapters around the Manson murders are absolutely devastating to read. What surprised me most is how darkly funny he can be in between the heavy moments. Most celebrity autobiographies skip the ugly parts or rely on a ghostwriter, but this feels like he actually sat down and wrote it himself, flaws and all. Yeah, it's long and occasionally self-indulgent, but honestly that's the point. If you're at all interested in his films or just European cinema history in general, this is absolutely worth your time. Way better than I expected.

Ordered this to reread the whole Dollanganger series since it had been years and I wanted to go through them again. Shipping was surprisingly fast, arrived two days earlier than expected. The book came in a padded mailer with cardboard backing so the corners weren't banged up at all, which I really appreciate since I'm picky about condition on my shelf. Opening it up, the cover art is even prettier in person and the pages feel good quality, not thin or flimsy. Started reading that same night and honestly couldn't put it down. Following Jory and Bart's adult lives and seeing how their parents' dark history still haunts the family is just as gripping now. Really glad I picked this one up. Solid copy, great delivery, no complaints.

The packaging is decent too, nothing has cracked or come loose during moves between rooms. That said, the actual content in these final episodes is just okay. The writing feels tired compared to earlier seasons and some plotlines are downright repetitive. The new cast members do their best but the chemistry never quite clicks the way it used to. It's watchable and completes the collection if you're a fan, just don't expect peak Hawaii Five-O. Three stars because the physical product is solid and durable, but the episodes themselves are mid-tier at best.

Basically this trap is a piece of folded cardboard with a sticky patch of goo on one side. You tear open a small plastic pouch to take out a little piece of white paper which is coated in the attractant, and drop it onto the sticky patch. The moths are supposed to fly into this little triangle area the size of a small slice of pie in search of the smell, and get stuck to the one sticky side. I guess it really depends on the types of moths you have floating around your house. We have the really tiny ones that come in from outside during spring and summer in California, and this didn't catch any of them. Hopefully that means they are not the type of moths that eat clothes.

I like Michael Crichton. A lot. I began a long time ago with the Andromeda Strain and that was a super thriller. Dragon Teeth is readable, but it is not a stand out and certainly way below the standard that made him one of the most popular author of thrillers. There are several reasons for this: 1) Crichton is generally excellent in giving you a deep understanding of the issues involved and leaves you enlightened and informed. For example, in Rising Sun he describes exactly how and why the Japanese company's surveillance cameras were superior and why American sleuths could not retrieve relevant images. In Disclosure he describes how the HR department functions and what is involved in cases of allegations of sexual harassment. Dragon Teeth is supposedly similar with paleontology as the backdrop. I disagree. The background provided is cursory and a fifteen minute read on the topic on Wikipedia will leave you better informed. 2) Crichton is excellent with cliffhangers. Each chapter leaves you waiting to see what happened next. The pace is relentless the climax is tremendous. Dragon Teeth is distinctly ho hum. The descriptions could come from a travel brochure, the characters are not well developed, and there is little to no suspense. In addition, there are loose ends. I will not say more because this would be a spoiler but one significant character introduced with fanfare leads you to believe that this character plays an important role in the tale. Instead this character then simply vanishes. 3) There are discrepancies which are jarring. For example, he describes the protagonist as having a full, luxuriant mustache. A sentence later he says that he had a clearly visible scar on his upper lip. The two are incompatible. He can have one or the other but not both. Overall, this is a reasonably good read but not a thriller and nowhere near the standard one would expect from Michael Crichton.

Amazing toy. Bought one for our 7 yr old niece and played around with it. Seems to be the hotness of 2013. But let's not lose our heads, here. It is merely a reconfigured Nerf Blaster. Instead of a trigger or a pump, you pretend to release a string. Aiming is more luck than skill. Comes with five whole darts. If they really want to learn about archery, you'd be better served buying a simple 'starter' bow and arrow set for about the same price. Teaches them a skill AND not to shoot their friends! My dad taught me to shoot when I was young and it is still a fun and relaxing hobby for me. This will not teach them anything, but dang, it's fun! Some advice on the price: -This retails for abouts twenty dollars. All my local retailers were sold out, and about two hours after purchasing it from Amazon the price here more than doubled. I see a lot of annoyed shoppers giving this one star over the price and that's bad form. You don't like the price, don't buy it. Don't mislead other shoppers about this great, fun product because the vender is gouging. Amazon isn't always the best price. I've seen a few 'hot' products sold for much cheaper locally (Target, WM, TrU) and while Amazon is great for most holiday shopping, in this case, at this moment, they aren't. ALWAYS remember to price check! -Don't be suckered into buying the 'girly' refills, the ones that match the colors of the bow. They cost more than twice the price of the regular orange ones! (Now THIS is some sexist gouging!) Kids don't care what color their ammo is and not everything girls touch needs to be pink. Save some money and pick up a pack of standard Nerf refills.

Marion Myles has an incredible talent for drawing raw emotion from the reader and giving an almost preternatural insight into the emotional state of her characters, to the point that you as the reader are living those emotions. Mental illness is a terrible and sad fact of life, and the ripple effects on the people around someone who suffers from a mental illness can be devastating in the extreme. Not everyone is lucky enough to have someone who cares enough to try to make a difference, but Annabelle is fortunate to have a group surrounding her that love her and will do whatever needs to be done, physically, mentally and emotionally to help her fight her demon Anorexia Nervosa. Falling in love is not something Annabelle thought would ever happen to her, while the 'Harpy' in her head constantly reinforced her feelings of worthlessness, but Alex fought for Belle and fought alongside her to defeat her disease and together they found an enduring love tempered in fire.

After the solid third season, Frasier came back with what could arguably be its best season. There were new storylines: Martin meeting a new lady, Sherry (played by Marsha Mason), who doesn't exactly hit it off with the rest of the gang--as well as continuations of old ones: Niles trying to reconcile with Maris. The biggest story of this season, though, was off-camera: star Kelsey Grammer went into rehab, which necessitated that the others in the cast pick up the slack. They proved up to the task, of course, and upon Grammer's return the show became better than ever. In this season, we were visited by familiar faces like Lilith and Frederick for a memorable Thanksgiving episode, as well as Agent Bebe, as well as surprise appearances (James Earl Jones). In addition, this season contained some of the truly greatest episodes of this show: the opener, The Two Mrs. Cranes, had the show's farcical sense of its humor at its most potent, Ham Radio featured delightfully unhinged performances by the supporting cast, and Are You Being Served? features one of the show's funniest moments. This is where the show hit its peak: the synthesis of great acting, writing, and ensemble acting that made this show memorable. If you love Frasier, these episodes are probably many of the reasons why. If you only buy one set of Frasier DVDs, this should probably be it.

I really hated Annabel's family with all my tiny heart and soul. And I did not understand why Annabel just didn't go work in the fields by herself and leave her family to their own devices. That was really odd and kind of annoying. Other than that, this book was decent enough. It's not the worst one I've ever read. I think there could have been a lot less bashing-you-over-the-head-with-religion, as in, it could have been much more subtly (and thus skillfully) done. Stephen's character could have been fleshed out a lot more. He was kind of just in the background for most of the time, and so the part that focused on him didn't have as much impact as it could have. I like le Wyse and I liked Annabel, but their relationship was a little "eh." I loved Eustacia but thought she talked like Yoda. Most of the language in this book was a little weirdly done. I get that the author was trying to evoke the 1300s, but she didn't pull it off unfortunately. It just came off weird and it was very distracting to read the characters talk like normal people and then say "Nay." It reminded me of that John Pinette sketch "I say nay nay!" I thought the storyline with the baliff was well done, though I could have lived without Gilbert and Adam's characters. Same with Beatrice - did that actually have a point or not really? This is a good, light read for when you just need a book that doesn't require your brain to function.

I grabbed this on a whim thinking it'd be another clunky Pride and Prejudice retelling with forced parallels and cringe-worthy dialogue. Honestly expected to ditch it by chapter three. Boy was I wrong. The author nails the modern prep school setting without making it feel like a Gossip Girl knockoff, and the sister dynamic between the two main characters actually feels genuine. What surprised me most was how the characters stand on their own, you don't need to be a Jane Austen superfan to enjoy this. The witty banter had me laughing out loud on my commute twice. My only complaint is that it ended too fast and now I need the sequel immediately. Way better than I anticipated.

This is the worst purchase I have ever made from Amazon. I have had the mattress for several days, opened and laying on my bed frame. It is supposed to be a 6 inch mattress and won't inflate beyond 4 inches. I got tired of sleeping in a recliner and tried to sleep on this thing last night. After an hour of being completely uncomfortable, I put my old two inch foam topper on it and tried sleeping again. No go. This thing gives and doesn't bounce back. It's like sleeping on a board. I am sore all over after my two attempts at trying to sleep. I am contacting Amazon for a return/refund. Don't waste your time buying thing thing, even for a guest room -- your guests will hate you if you do! And, when it was opened, there was a stink in the room coming off the mattress. It off gassed for two days. I received this in Nov, 2017 and the tag on the mattress said it was made in China in Sept, 2016. So it was manufactured, then rolled up tight for over a year. No wonder it doesn't inflate. I am very, very disappointed in this. UPDATE: I returned the mattress to Amazon. There was no hastle, arranged for UPS to pick it up and the refund to my account was issued within 2 hours of being picked up. The hardest part about the return was rolling up the mattress (it was very stiff in the middle), tying it off, wrapping it up in the plastic it came in and then taping card board all around the plastic. I probably didn't have to do all of that but I did it anyway, just to be sure it arrived in one piece. Don't waste your money on this product.

Where's a Zero rating when you need one? This is just... a mess. Overrun with mean-spiritedness, the vileness of the anti-female ramblings is matched only by the sub-amateur production values. The writing is awful. The camerawork is headache-inducing. The acting would look bad on the stage of the worst gosapel stage show you ever saw. Obviously, renting a camcorder and tripod from the local camera store does NOT qualify you as the next Spike Lee. If this is the best you can do, you're not even the next Matty Rich! How bad is this movie? Take the "skeletons in the closet" rant from "Eddie Murphy: Raw" and multiply it by 1,000! Every woman these guys know is a "farm tool," "witch with a B in front" or worse. There's not one redeeming female character. There's not even one redeeming MALE character! These are familiar faces, but these actors can do better than this. Based on these performances, they should really reconsider their career goals. If Winky Dinky Dog won't take them back, there's always work at the post office.

Bought these on a whim because the price was so reasonable and honestly didn't expect much. What a pleasant surprise. They're my go-to slip-on for everything now. Morning coffee runs, grabbing the mail, walking the dog around the block. The sole is substantial enough that wet pavement isn't an issue, which is more than I can say for other cheap options I've tried. The lining is still intact after five months of daily abuse, and they haven't gone flat like most sub-thirty-dollar shoes do. For what I paid, these punch way above their weight. Already planning to grab another pair as backup.

I bought this knife after seeing tons of positive reviews on the Internet. I wasn't disappointed. The blade deploys very smoothly with one hand and is razor sharp out of the box. For me, it's a bit too large and heavy to use as my every day carry knife. My trusty Swiss Army knife has nothing to worry about there. However, when I'm out hiking or camping, this knife will definitely be going along. Overall, this is a great knife for the money.

Adhesive isn't great (using in a vehicle where the temperature varies significantly). The opening is also too wide for a single charging cable (iphone) and will not hold it in place. Probably works fine for indoor use on larger diameter cables.

I've had this remote for about 8 months now and it's been dropped more times than I can count. Still works perfectly. The build quality is honestly impressive compared to the flimsy remotes that came with my TV and soundbar. After using it daily, the buttons haven't gotten sticky or worn out at all. Setup took maybe 15 minutes the first time, and I haven't needed to reconnect it to my computer since. The battery life is solid, I've only swapped them out once. My wife wasn't sure about having another remote in the house but now she reaches for this one first every time. Only minor complaint is I wish the volume buttons were a bit bigger. Great purchase overall, planning to grab another one for the bedroom setup.

I foster pregnant guinea pigs and go through an absurd amount of bedding. These washable pads changed everything for me. The absorbency is incredible, way better than any fleece liner I've tried, and there's zero smell even after a full day with my messy sows. I just shake them out into the trash and toss them in the wash. They've held up perfectly after two months of weekly washing with no fraying or shrinking. The waterproof backing actually works, unlike cheaper pads that wick moisture right through to the floor. If you run a rescue, breed, or just have a few pigs at home, do yourself a favor and stock up. I've already ordered four more. Only wish they came in bigger sizes for my C&C cages.
The variable speed dial is a game changer. No more messy blowouts when starting a bead. Battery lasts forever and cleanup takes seconds. Best tool purchase I've made all year.

The overall quality of this blade is astounding. Buck never fails to deliver an awesome knife product. This knife has quickly become my favorite hunting knife. The feel of the blade in your hand is great. It has good balance and is as sharp as a razor. I was even surprised at the high quality of leather sheath it comes with. One day when I'm old I will be passing this blade down to one of my children.

Got these for my pantry and love how clear they are. Can see exactly what's inside without pulling each one down. The latching lids are super secure, I accidentally dropped a full one and nothing popped open. Fit perfectly on my wire shelves too. Grabbed four more after seeing how well they worked.

I've had this top for about six months now and it's become my most reached-for closet staple. The fit is spot on, fitted enough to look put together but stretchy enough to be comfortable all day at work. I've machine washed it probably fifteen times and the fabric still looks brand new with zero shrinking or pilling. The neckline sits perfectly without gaping, and I love that I can dress it up with a blazer or wear it casual with jeans. Honestly worth every penny and I'm about to order two more colors before they sell out.
Unfortunately the articulation makes the figure's top half look a little freakish. But If that doesn't bother you, you get a lot of playability and displayability with that articulation.
We have enjoyed the first two episodes very much. I lol forward to watching more.

I said just what I needed, and I meant it. This book goes into a lot of detail, however I was particularly interested in the sections on plants that work well, gardening bed ideas, the tools, and reading about the disadvantages. I live in a small but tall building with plenty of room on the roof. The space is not shared, so my wife has always wanted to grow her own vegetables on the roof. She got the idea from an article about urban agriculture, and she always wanted to know more. After I read the book, it was clear that it is perfectly possible, without the disadvantage of the cost mentioned. In my opinion it is affordable, and this book really showed me how. If you are in a similar situation to me, I would recommend reading this to see just how viable raised garden beds are. Thanks.
Only used it once over the last 5 months, but it didn't disappoint. It was actually in a race, so instead of losing 10 minutes on a flat, after 3 minutes I was on the saddle again.
Awesome glass's...Everybody liked them so much I ended up ordering several for the guys and gals at work.
I love the light feature! Easy on and off and easy to read. What more can you ask for?
Material feels cheap and they slide right down my calves within minutes.
Some rope toys are too thin for large dogs. This one is a thicker rope toy and offers hours a tug related fun for one of my dogs. These work well to floss dogs teeth if they like to chew on them.
Our dog loves these. They lasted a long time considering they were chewed on all of the time.

Bought this for my 4 year old's birthday and it's been a huge hit. The shoulder strap is the real selling point, she trots around the house with it everywhere. Holds a surprising amount of those tiny pony figures and their accessories, which keeps them off my floor. Came with one pony included, nice little extra. Solid quality for the price.
Very easy to set up and use. These look great around our garden!

The rubber itself is seriously thick and durable, but the straight valve design makes these basically unusable on my Trikke. You have to angle the stem way over just to get a pump head on there, and after a few fills the rubber just rips right at the base. Mine leaked overnight after just three weeks of use.

Bought this to replace the terrible built-in speakers on my Samsung and honestly couldn't be happier with the whole thing. The wireless subwoofer paired up immediately without any fuss and I had everything running in maybe ten minutes. The difference during movie night was immediately noticeable, dialogue comes through crisp and clear while explosions actually have some real weight behind them now. I've got it sitting on my media console and it fits perfectly without looking bulky. The remote is straightforward and I like that I can tweak the bass level depending on what I'm watching. Music streaming over Bluetooth from my phone works flawlessly too. For what I paid, this absolutely exceeded my expectations and I'd recommend it to anyone wanting better sound without a complicated setup.

Grabbed this on a Kindle deal and honestly it's worth way more than I paid. The dynamic between the two leads keeps you guessing the whole way through. Fast-paced, entertaining, and leaves you wanting the next book immediately. Great pickup for thriller fans.
Solid little driver for the money. Swapped out three pocket clip screws in minutes. No complaints.

Honestly wasn't expecting much for the price but this thing blew me away. Picks up signals way better than my old unit ever did. Installation took five minutes and it's been rock solid since day one. Way beyond what I thought I was getting.

I've run about 400 board feet of hard maple and walnut through this thing over the past six months and it just keeps going. No snipe issues, and the knives are still cutting clean with no tearout. The infeed and outfeed tables haven't loosened up at all. For the money, this thing is built like a tank and takes a real beating.

I've reread this thing at least four times since buying it last year and the digital file holds up perfectly across all my devices. Still a total comfort read. Great for unwinding after long workdays.

Got this for my daughter's Pokemon birthday and it was a huge hit. The balloon is big, colorful, and the printing is sharp, not cheap looking at all. We had it inflated at the grocery store and it floated for over two weeks without deflating. Definitely recommend to any parent planning a Pokemon party. Pair it with some themed plates and you're set. Way better than the generic options at the party store.

My 2 mastiffs and a shepherd love these...it took them about an hour to remove the marrow and strip the jerky off the bone, but even after the good stuff is gone, they love to chew on the bones. Nice smokey, bbq smell. Not too messy. If you can find them for an average of $3 per bone, that's a good deal. I was lucky and had free shipping on my order. I've already reordered another case!!!
Got these for my wife's restaurant job three months ago. Still holding up great after tons of washing. No shrinking or fading at all.
Bought this for my 2 year old daughter. Perfect size for her to reach everything. Would definitely recommend to parents of toddlers!

I'm 24, 19 weeks pregnant with baby #3. When I am not pregnant I do workout videos and run. This video is way too old school for me. I need something that actually makes me feel like I'm getting a work out and its worth it. I did break a little sweat doing this but I was just hoping for more. I know its not going to be like my other workout videos that I have for non pregnant people but I just think that in this day in age they could come up with some different techniques then just salsa dancing and doing the mamba....
Been using these constantly for months and they still look brand new. No cracking or loosening. They seal tightly on every bottle I've tried and dispense just the right amount each time.
Way better than I expected. The story pulls you in right away and doesn't let go. Couldn't stop until I finished every single book.

We have 4 cats. They all seem to love this litter. They go in, dig around awhile, do their duty and then they spend a more than usual time scratching around in the litter box before they exit. We, at first, though that it was because they were having a difficult time covering up their duty with the light litter. We were wrong. We have two litter boxes downstairs (and two upstairs) sitting side-by-side so we performed an experiment on the two downstairs litters. We put light litter in the left hand box and clay litter in the right. In the morning we filled one litter with their usual clay based litter and one with this light litter. The cats used them all day. At the end of the first day the box with the light litter was used about 30% more than the clay. On the second day the light litter box was use almost exclusively. There were only a couple of pee clumps in the clay litter. On the third day we reversed the positions of the two litter boxes. Now they were clay left and light right. Only the right box (light litter) was used all day. The clay litter box was clean! Both upstairs clay litter boxes were pretty much unused except for some pee. I have since seen the cats standing in line waiting to use the light litter box while the clay litter box was being left unused and completely vacant. Darndest thing I've ever seen. Now we're putting clay in the bottom and light on top until the clay is gone. From then on, light litter ONLY. Oh yeah, the light litter does not track-out on the cat's paws and then onto the floor nearly as much as the clay litter. This makes my wife very happy.

Honestly expected a lot more from this. The sounds are pretty limited and it just sits there unless you buy the other pieces to go with it. My kid played with it for about five minutes before moving on to something else. The phrases it says are kind of weird for a kids toy too. Build quality seems fine for the price, but I was hoping it would stand alone better as its own thing. Probably worth picking up if you're planning to get the full collection, otherwise skip it.
I put this on a sandwich, it tastes terrible. Looks like mayo, but it's not. Do not eat this. Works great for sealing leaks though.

For the price, this is it man. The cards do sometimes fall out when i open it, so keep it facing up when you open it. I thought 8 cards would be enough, but I quickly found that i needed a bigger case...so if you have a small gig where water may come into play, get it.

Finally stopped finding scattered litter all over my hallway. The honeycomb design grabs the pieces right off their paws before they make it past the mat. Cleanup takes seconds, just vacuum or dump it back in the pan. My only gripe is the surface feels a bit stiff underfoot, and the edges curl slightly after a month. Still, perfect for anyone with indoor cats who track everywhere.
Excellent quality, you get what you pay for! Heavy gage steel, the padding is excellent and most of all the fit and finish is 100% spot on. I really love this machine!
Arrived two days early in a sturdy box with no damage. Fired it up right away and the screen looks amazing. Super happy so far.
The footbed on these is amazing, plenty of arch support for all-day walking. My only complaint is the toe post rubbed a bit the first few days until they broke in. Would buy again.

Got these a few weeks ago and I'm honestly impressed. The sound quality is way better than I expected for the price, decent bass, clear highs, and they don't get all muddy when you crank the volume. Pairing took about ten seconds with my phone and they reconnect automatically every time I pull them out of the case. Battery life has been solid too, I get through a full workday of podcasts and calls without needing to top up. Call quality on the other end is apparently good since nobody's complained about hearing me. The touch controls took a day to get used to but work fine once you figure out the tap zones. For what these cost, I really can't complain. Would definitely buy again.

Was hesitant to drop money on an SSD but at this price point it's honestly a steal. Swapped out the old spinning disk in my laptop and the change is night and day. Boot times went from nearly two minutes to maybe fifteen seconds. Apps that used to hang forever load instantly now. Even my wife noticed how much snappier everything feels, and she could care less about computer specs. Installation was painless, just cloned my old drive and swapped it in. Been running it hard for about three weeks now with zero issues. The cost per gigabyte keeps getting better and honestly you can't beat the performance jump for what these are going for lately. Skip the coffee for a week and buy one, totally worth every penny.

Got about 100 pages in and lost interest. The stories start blending together after a while. If you're obsessed with rock memoirs and want every single backstage anecdote from that era, you'll probably enjoy this. But casual readers looking for something deeper or more reflective should pass. Not terrible, just felt like I'd read it all before in better books.

The setting at The Beaudelaire was nicely done, felt atmospheric without being over the top. I liked the tension between the three characters, but the pacing felt rushed in the second half. Things escalated too quickly between Brent, Eve, and Remy without enough emotional groundwork. The New Year's Eve backdrop was a fun touch and added some urgency to the story. Steamy scenes were well written, just wish the resolution didn't feel so abrupt. Decent short read if you want something fast, but don't expect deep character development.
Arrived quickly in a padded mailer so no bent corners. Dimitri's storyline grabbed me right away, really solid entry in the series, though not perfect.

For SyFy, this isn't worse than anyother tripe they put out. It's got Ving Rhames and Lesley-Ann Brandt is pretty kickass. With one exception, the cast is actually pretty good. Which leads us to-Taryn Manning. She has to be the WORST actress in the history of zombie films. She can't act her way out of a paperbag. Her character, Romona was also the most useless character I have ever seen in a movie. She gives "too stupid to live" a whole new meaning. Her friend is being murdered, what does she do? RUN AWAY and yell at everyone else because they didn't save him! The special effects are sub-par, but I kind of expected that. There were a few major errors that would have been cheap and made things look more realistic, but they producers were obviously too lazy to figure out. For instance Henry kills a zombie with a chainsaw, then right after he stands there holding the chainsaw and you can clearly see THERE IS NO CHAIN on it! How hard would have been to use a fake prop chain or put a chain on after the "kill". The film moves along fine until the end and SPOILER alert: there are not one but TWO freaky, fake as f@!& looking zombie tigers at the dock. It was like they didn't know how to end the movie, so they just started throwing crap out there to see what would stick.
Although I love Sue Grafton's novels, I felt this one was not up to her usual standard. The stories were disjointed and the ending was abrupt. Still worth reading but not her strongest story.

Paid $4.99 for this and honestly expected more. The story drags in the middle and I found myself skimming whole chapters just to get to anything interesting. The characters are flat and the dialogue feels forced. I've read plenty of books in this genre for way less that were significantly better written. Not the worst thing I've read but definitely not worth what they're charging. Glad I didn't spend more on it.

I'm a 75 year old and assembled this product myself. I sat down, placed all the pieces on the floor, in order and simply followed the directions. I used a hammer covered with a sock and it worked just like a rubber mallet. I didn't want to make holes in my bathroom wall, so this product works perfectly for me. It looks modern and matches with all the bronze accessories in my guest bathroom and have gotten compliments from guests already. Thanks Amazon. I always find what I need at Amazon. Packaging and delivery were great too.
Nice bed. Going on 2 years now. The dogs prefer this one to the other two I have. Good bang for the buck.
Way way way better than I thought they would be. Good weight, sharp blade and cool graphic. Perfect gift for a zombie lover!!
THIS PRODUCT IS SIMPLY AMAZING AND SO HELPFUL!!! I am always screwing up the tips of screws when i am doing woodworking! LOVE this!!!!!
The plot is fast paced, the scenery logical, the activities legal and illegal ring true. Overall, this is an extremely good book Kudos to the author.

Been buying these for about six months now and my cat goes absolutely bonkers every time I open the bag. She hears the crinkling from across the house and comes sprinting. What I really appreciate is that they're not just empty treats, her breath has noticeably improved since we started using them daily, and her vet even commented on how clean her back teeth looked at her last checkup. The texture is key because she actually has to chew them instead of just swallowing whole like she does with everything else. A little pricey compared to basic treats, but honestly worth every penny for the dental benefit alone. My only complaint is the bags could be bigger because we go through them fast. Highly recommend for picky cats.

Got this for my 70lb shepherd mix and it's held up beautifully after three months of daily use. The dual-ball design gives it enough weight for a solid game of fetch in the yard, and that multi-tone squeaker drives him absolutely bonkers in the best way. The nylon shell is tough, we've had some intense tug-of-war sessions and not a single seam has come apart. I'd definitely recommend this to anyone with a large, high-energy dog who destroys typical plush toys in minutes. Only downside is the squeaker can get annoying after a while, but seeing how happy he is makes it worth it.
Very soft and pliable but smaller than realized. Curled up it fits in hand. Really soft and not hard plastic so was pleased with that and detail was nice.

This bike does seem sturdy, but came with so many paint chips!! Pretty disappointing. Some chips appear to have already been badly touched up with not-quite-matching touch up paint, and others aren't fixed at all. I read this in other reviews for this bike, so I guess it's quite common. The chipped paint didn't occur in transit, as the bike was very well protected in foam, cardboard and plastic. The bike still functions and went together fine, but don't expect the paint to be in good condition. I snapped a few pictures for examples, but there were a lot more than what's pictured.
Way better than I expected. Silva's latest Allon thriller keeps you hooked from page one.

Comfortable to wear all day and the draw is smooth once you break it in. Mine sat a little high on the belt but that's personal preference. Retention is solid without needing extra straps. Would buy again.
Perfect for goody bags at my daughter's safari birthday. Would recommend to any teacher or parent!

Did a lot of looking before purchasing. Teva is a very good brand and has a lot of different styles. I was looking for something that could do double duty on sand, water and walking. I liked the color combination of this one along with the style. Has the ability to make the instep area tighter or looser as necessary and the heel area is velcro and can be lengthened or shortened if need be. The strap across the foot is also ajustable for making just the right fit. I have a slight issue on the inside near the big toe area where my toe nail hits a seam but I'm hoping that additional wear will break them in. (and yes I have clipped my nails!) Cute and comfy.
Got this for my old arthritic lab last winter and she spends hours on it every day. Warms up nicely and I haven't had any issues with durability. Wish the cord was a little longer though.

Picked up two of these cases for under ten bucks each and honestly couldn't be happier. The padding is decent enough for basic storage and transport, and the latches feel secure without being hard to open. Fits my scoped rifle perfectly with room to spare. You really can't beat this level of protection at such a low price point.

Arrived two days early in a slightly battered box, but the shoes inside were fine. Loved the color right out of the wrapper. Put them on and walked around the block, comfortable from the start, though the arch support could be a bit better. Solid first impression overall.
Very easy to install this on our new Fire HD7. Clear and works well to protect the screen.
Used these lights at Christmas to light our walkway. They looked great and two sets attached together easily.

Didn't expect much going in since I grabbed this during a free promo, but it turned out to be a genuinely solid read. The whole chase dynamic keeps things moving at a good clip, and I actually cared about what happened to Ben and his mates, which isn't always a given with teen sci-fi. The way the story jumps around in time took a few chapters to get used to, but once it clicked, the reveals landed pretty well. My one gripe is that some of the supporting characters could've used more page time, especially Ben's friend group. Felt like I only got surface-level stuff from them. Still, the mystery builds nicely and the pacing rarely drags. Better than I anticipated for sure. Would pick up a sequel.
A fun twist on an old, old legend. A modern, cautionary fairy tale. Share it with your kids and friends.
Awesome probiotic! My shepherd has a sensitive stomach and this helps keep him regulated and if he ever gets into something he shouldn't, this helps him handle it.
Interesting realistic look at Dublin music scene. Amazing for a low-budget movie and mostly non-actors.

These turned my cat's bowl into a murky mess within two days. The filter literally started shedding brown flakes into the water. I'd only recommend this to someone who enjoys wasting money on garbage. Switched back to the Petco brand and the difference is night and day. Save yourself the headache and skip these completely.
good memories of an old program I used to enjoy. Acting and stories are a bit dated but I enjoyed them nonethelessa

Grabbed this on a Kindle deal and definitely got my money's worth. The action scenes hit hard and Bubba's crew doesn't mess around. Some of the jokes landed better than others, but for under three bucks I can't complain. Solid entertainment for a weekend read.

For the price, this purse is amazing. Fits my phone, compact, lip balm, and keys with zero bulk. The material feels way more expensive than what I paid. Shipping was quick too. Definitely recommend grabbing one!
My dog loves her new bed. A very nice thickness to be super cozy. This is a great dog bed and a great price point!!
I've been shooting with this for eight months now, and it still works like new. Survived rain, drops, and heavy travel without a single issue.

I purchased this as an extra battery for my Canon SD1400 IS after a week-long camping trip in the mountains of Southern Utah. The original battery never ran out of power, but I couldn't review all the photos and 720p movies because I was concerned about using all the original battery power. This battery is identical to the original and is a genuine Canon battery. Works and looks just like the original battery that came with the camera. The battery was shipped two weeks sooner than the estimated delivery date. It arrived packaged securely in good condition.

Been using this daily for about six months now and it's held up great. Sweat, rain, showers, still ticking. Battery still gets me through a full week. Syncing is reliable and the band hasn't cracked or stretched out. Solid little tracker for the price.
My cat loves this - she thinks she is the queen sitting on the top. She plays with the toy and uses the scratching post. It looks nice too.
It's a nice viewer when it works, you can't turn it on and off if the SD card is in it causing you to hit the reset button every time.
Great cup. Will last forever. Keeps things cool / warm.
Have bought many Nerf guns for my kids, and this has the poorest performance.
Been using this for months with my heavy-chewing lab and it's barely scratched. Tough material, totally worth it.
Paid way too much for what's basically a rock on a string. The jade looks cheap and dull, nothing like the photos. Not worth half the price.
This clamp works great until you strap on a bigger flash. It struggles a bit with the 580ex II but works great with the 430ex series.

Dim light on after unlock and activated the solar light. the light gets brighter when detected motion. Size of the light is 3 inches by 4 inches included 2 screw, can mount with one screw at the center top of light. Price is comparable to similar products.
Honestly didn't expect much since my shepherd destroys everything, but this thing has survived two weeks of constant chewing. Way exceeded what I hoped for.
Fascinating update on Joanna Russ' world populated only by women. Feels real, no nonsense, great world-bulding. A little humorless. But otherwise terrific story-telling.

Bought this on a whim after seeing it mentioned in a forum and honestly didn't expect much. Turns out I stayed up until 2am two nights in a row finishing it. The writing just pulls you in without being pretentious or overly dense, which I really appreciated. I'd specifically recommend this to anyone who's been in a reading slump lately. It's the kind of book that reminds you why you enjoy reading in the first place. Also great for book clubs, there's plenty here to discuss without it feeling like homework. My sister doesn't read much fiction at all and even she couldn't put it down when I lent her my copy last week. Just go ahead and order it. You won't regret it.

This is my third one of these! Comfortable and very warm. Only drawback is the exterior snags and rips easily. The first two lasted me only 1 season because I got them caught on fencing or other things around the farm. Ive sewed them up and use them for daily work and save this one for going out and about.

Got these for my office job where I'm on my feet most of the day, and honestly they've become my go-to pair. The construction is seriously solid, no flimsy feeling like some other brands I've tried that fall apart after a few wears. These have real heft to them without being clunky or uncomfortable. I've worn mine through full nine-hour shifts with zero issues. The finish holds up nicely too, even after getting caught in the rain a couple times walking to my car. I'd specifically recommend these to anyone working in retail, healthcare, or an office setting where you need to look put-together but can't sacrifice comfort. My sister-in-law is a teacher and I'm already planning to grab her a pair for Christmas. Worth every penny honestly.

Fits great and is very comfortable, however after wearing it one time, I ended up getting a hole in one of the sleeves just doing regular thimgs around the house. I was disappointed it is not as durable for the cost..
Really cozy throw that fits perfectly on my couch. It does leave some fuzz on clothes but keeps me warm.

My favorite game of all time! This game is so much fun for adults and kids alike. Some of the younger kids might need a little help but most of them were able to do just fine. You can even always make sure kids get the villager card. I strongly recommend watching a demonstration on youtube on how to play this. (The game seems complicated but don't be afraid. The best part is that you don't have to memorize anything that the characters do - the narrator tells you as you're playing. ) We tell the kids that they are allowed to lie when they play the game and it kind of worries them to lie at first but then they really get into it and have a lot of fun.:) You also use the one night ultimate Werewolf free app with this, that narrates the game for you as you go. You can customize it on the app as much as you'd like, including music, the voice, and you can decide which characters to activate during the game. You can also slow the game down significantly for kids or for those learning to play the first time. You can actually download the one night ultimate werewolf app before you buy the game to kind of check it out. Best. Game.

Having enjoyed the first 'Elizabeth' so much, I was prepared to be disappointed in the second. It was reviewed as more concerned with costuming than acting. Call me crazy, I loved it. Cate Blanchett is a powerful Elizabeth. At no point did I feel the costumes outweighed the cast. While the first movie is still my favorite, I wouldn't have missed this.
Got this for my aggressive chewer and the textured bumps are a game changer. Keeps his teeth clean and he hasn't managed to tear it apart yet. Definitely worth the money.
Again it just goes to prove once in true love ever a divorce can't stop what God wants for both of them.

recieved free for honest review 60kg for medium sized dogs retractable dog leash. Made of environmentally protection material TPE. ABS has a nice rubber grasp that is easy to hold and your hand does not slip off. you press down on the black button to lock and release to let it go. 4 meter cord. the light takes three triple a batteries ( you have to provide those) the light is very bright which I love. I live in the country so it is dark at night to take out the dog it is nice to have a built in light I do not have to search for. you can also take the light off if you want and hold it in your hand. I like to leave it as one piece so I don't lose it. really well made hard durable plastic and the end of the leash has light reflector so you can be seen in the dark by cars. I like this one very much and my pomerian who is three pounds can use it just fine.

I had compared the dimensions on the site to a fleece jacket I had and the Condor came up a little short (less than 1/2 inch though) in each dimension compared to the fleece dimensions. I ordered the 3X (there is no 4X). It is a nice jacket, I just do not like the way it drapes on the body. The Spandex does not really allow it drape anyway. I am 6 feet plus and about 260 pounds. It does fit snugly but I think it is really the Spandex. If I could relax the Spandex a bit, I would really like this jacket. I love the number of pockets, especially the fore arm pocket. I use it for my ID card. I love the hood with the visor. I wear it to work over a tee shirt and polo shirt normally. I would say it would be comfortable no lower than the high 40s F by itself. You could extend that if you wore a fleece or sweater underneath. It is more comfortable in the 50s F though. I have worn it in some pretty good wind and rain storms. Wind intrusion is not a problem. So far, the rain has not penetrated the material either. The slash pockets are a little high up for me though. It could be an inch or 2 longer in the overall length. It could also be a bit more packable. I wanted it to roll up in the bottom of my work back pack for when I travel. Does not really lend itself to that though.
Arrived very fast with most ants alive. About 2-3 were dead upon arrival. But otherwise they are functioning well, digging their tunnels and quite interesting to watch.
The arch support is surprisingly good for the price, but the toe box runs narrow. After a few hours my feet were aching. Decent enough for short errands but not all-day wear.

I've been following Buroker's work for years and getting to revisit this world through fresh eyes was such a treat. What really stood out to me here is how she handles the new protagonist's voice, distinct from Amaranthe but still carrying that same sharp wit and underdog energy I've come to love. The banter is top notch as always, and the pacing never drags. I specifically appreciated how the worldbuilding from the original series gets woven in without feeling like you need a recap. Longtime fans will catch the references but new readers won't feel lost. My only tiny gripe is I wanted more page time with a couple of the side characters, but that's just me being greedy. Already counting down to the next installment.

Simple and easy to use. Set it and leave it. Been working great running my 2400GPM pump motor. I installed mine inside the home and I can turn it on anytime or the clock time will. Keeps my fountain water clear as I sue Bromide tabs that require water moving to work; three hours total a day in 1 hour segments.

I've had this pump for almost two years now and it's still going strong. I use it regularly to clear water from my low spot in the yard after heavy storms, plus I've loaned it to my brother twice for his crawlspace. It's been worked hard and dirty, dropped on concrete, left out in weather more times than I should admit, and it just keeps running. The motor doesn't overheat like cheaper ones I've tried before, and the housing hasn't cracked or shown any wear worth mentioning. Starts right up every single time I plug it in. Honestly didn't expect much for the price but this thing has outlasted two other brands combined. If it ever does die I'll buy the exact same one again without hesitation.

Picked this up before a trip to Yellowstone and the zoom on this thing blew me away. Got crisp, detailed shots of bison from what felt like a mile away. Way better than my old point-and-shoot. The menu takes a little getting used to but once you figure it out, it's smooth sailing. Definitely worth the money if you want great photos without hauling around a bunch of lenses.

I acquired this amazing product for cheap from BestBuy, because I think Dyson was renaming their DC series to "V6" at the time. In the past, the thought of dealing with cords and heavy vacuums typically prevailed over my urge to keep things clean, until such a time when everything just became intolerably dusty. However, with this vacuum, cleaning is actually kind of fun. But what really inspired me to write this review was how awesomely and effectively this thing sucks up and kills bugs. Turn the "Max" setting on and no insect or arachnid shall escape its wrath. Suck them up from five feet away, spin them around and they are dead. I love this thing.

Arrived two days earlier than expected and the box was in great shape, no dents or crushed corners like I've gotten from other online tool orders. Everything was snug inside with no shifting during transit. Pulled the saw out and the first thing I noticed is how light it feels compared to my old corded Makita. The grip is comfortable and the blade guard moves smoothly without sticking. Popped in a freshly charged 20V battery and ripped through some 2x6 pine scraps I had sitting in the garage no problem. Plenty of power for a cordless this size. Only used it for one weekend so far but I'm really impressed right out of the gate. Great value if you're already in the B&D 20V ecosystem.

Ordered this on a whim after watching some Rathbone Holmes clips online. Shipped fast and arrived in a sturdy case with no cracks or damage. The disc was secure inside and played perfectly right away. Picture quality is way better than I expected for something this old, nice and crisp on my TV. Only watched the first two films so far but really enjoying the vibe. Nigel Bruce's Watson is goofier than I remembered but it works. Solid pickup for classic mystery fans.

Arrived two days early in a padded mailer with no cracks or damage to the case. Popped it in right away and the picture quality is way better than I expected for the price. Creepy atmosphere hits you immediately. Super happy with this purchase.
Excellent place to buy fast delivery, item as illustrated, and good price, is what we all expect when making a purchase.

Okay, it's true. I love Fannie Flagg books. Her characters are delightful, engaging, and quirky, but always memorable!. This summer, I've been reading some pretty heavy books about WWII, so I needed something light and fun. I turned to Flagg and her book about Elner Shimfissle. Just from the name of the main character alone, you know you are in for a fun time. Elner touched so many lives for good over the course of her long life that her death, its aftermath, and visit to heaven, make for some laugh out loud reading. I recommend this book to anyone who likes implausible, humorous books with feel good results and characters.
Got these for the gym and the grip is unreal on rope climbs. Sizing runs a half size small so order up. Solid build for the money.

I love the print of this dog harness. It makes my male dog (min pin) look extremely masculine. I've had other dog owners take notice of it and they've mentioned that it loos extremely stylish. My one tiny gripe with it and the reason it gets 4 stars from me is the fact that it ran a little small. With a few adjustments i was able to get it to fit properly.
Great purchase! No mess! Works perfect. Our pup has a beautiful coat! Vet recommended to brush him daily and his coat is very shiny.

Got this for my niece's birthday and she absolutely loves it. The little compartments are perfect for holding her treasures and hair clips. Quality seems solid for the price. She carries it everywhere now.
Brim is ridiculously flimsy. Folded and creased right out of the package. Total waste.
I drove 100 miles an hour And it stayed on
Fast shipping. Deck was sealed.

I've had this on my Kindle for about three months now and I keep going back to reread certain chapters. That says a lot because I usually delete books after finishing them. The writing really holds up after multiple reads, you catch things you missed the first time around. Edwards branching out into a different style was a solid move. Some of the pacing in the middle dragged a bit for me, hence the four stars instead of five, but the character work is strong and the world she built feels lived in. My Kindle app has synced this across my phone and tablet with zero issues, bookmarks save properly, no formatting glitches. If you're on the fence, just grab it. Already preordered the next one.
Great fabric and colour. Turned out exactly as I had expected. Also did not lose any colour upon washing.

These are good and work well. I wanted to add a compressed air outlet in my garage soldering a lot of copper pipe joints. I cut the 3/4" copper line and pushed this fitting on. Then I pushed the end of a 20.' piece of "PEX" 1/2" - flexiable plastic pipe or tubing in the other end. I ran the PEX down a center column in my garage and to a new air outlet. Now I have a air outlet in the center of the garage. Much faster than rigid copper pipe. I avoided 20 solder joints on 10 copper fittings. Most in the overhead working off a ladder. PEX is usually used for water lines but it works well for air lines too.
Bought this for small household fixes and it handles everything. Lightweight, comfortable grip, and the battery lasts way longer than I expected. Totally worth it.
High Quality, Gives an Upgraded look to my wall sockets.

Band is unbelievably small, if you have large breasts but have the width of a stick, you should be able to fit into it nicely. Very disappointing. Material is awfully cheap, the knots in the back that hold your straps are plastic... One good tug and it snaps and breaks.
I was looking for a two point sling for my D.D. AR. Found one on Amazon at a great price. So I ordered it. I got it and I am very happy with it.

I picked this up on a whim and ended up reading it in one sitting. The thing that really sold me was how authentic Annalise's devotion to ballet felt. You can tell the author actually understands the discipline and physical toll of professional dance training, the blisters, the obsessiveness, the way every decision revolves around the next performance. That detail made her character feel real instead of like some generic protagonist who just happens to mention dancing now and then. The romance builds at a good pace too, nothing rushed or ridiculous. Raphael is well written and their dynamic works without being cheesy. My only tiny complaint is I wanted more from the ending, but honestly that is just me being greedy. Definitely recommend this one.

If you pay attention to the type and cut of the blade. If you look at this tool closely, you will notice that it sharpens all blades the same way, but not all garden tools have the same style blade. Also, given that garden tools have heavier tougher blades, this tool does sometimes loosen up during rigorous sharpening. I use a handheld stone to clean up the blade a bit before using this sharpener. But, for the price and convenience it's a reasonable tool sharpener.
Arrived quickly and sealed tight. Pellets are pretty big but seem decent quality for the price. Works fine so far.
The pattern is adorable and my dog gets compliments, but the fabric has zero structure. It wrinkles immediately and the hem drags because it won't hold its shape at all.
I thoroughly enjoyed reading this book. It was a very believable story and my heart went out to those women.

I found the book to be very interesting and agree for the most part with the author. However, I am not sure ALL murderers have had a bad upbringing where they were whipped or even abused. I am sure there are cases where this is not so. It would be interesting to examine more cases especially here in America. I was raised by a half German and half English mom and was not abused much. I turned out to be a happy and productive person. I do not blame my mom for her abuses which were there but nobody is perfect and she grew up with the threat of the strap herself. I do not see the purpose in blaming parents except in the cases described in the book. To me I wonder if it is more of a German problem but I am probably nave to think that. As stated in the book it is not limited to just one country. I know from the parenting books out now (since I am a parent to two girls myself) in America the opposite is the case. It is a fear to even raise a hand to a child because of the consequences. Which is good. My sister has perfectly mannered children who have never been harmed physically and I hope emotionally as well. Although I do not know. My children are not well behaved but they are allowed to be children and I try not to be permissive but it happens. A very quick read for I read it in less than a week. Nice read. It kept me entertained. A little too much on Hitler though and I did not care to read about the child murderer either.

I thought my metal table top was ready to use after one day of drying. Wrong! Cure time is 7 days! I have use this product on other outdoor metal furniture. It does OK stopping rust. I have also used the spay-on black Hammerite which seems to be better at stopping rust.
These saved my fingers for sure. Very easy to use and was able to load quickly. Came in very handy when I loaded my four new magazines which were tight.

I bought our Bosch mixer in 1970 and own most of the attachments that are available for it. This is an industrial grade product; it's indestructible. The criticism that it will not work well for small batches is totally wrong. I've been using it, on a regular basis, to make single loaves of bread for 40 years. This is an idea that has been spread by people who own a Kitchen Aid mixer. It is just not true! And all the attachments including the blender, food processor, juicer, grinder work beautifully.

I've read a number of true crime books, but haven't delved too much into movies. I was actually steered towards this one - which I'd never heard of - by a review on another movie, so three cheers to Amazon reviewers for helpful recommendations! The movie intrigued me for a number of reasons - true crime, drama, and John Cusack playing a character other than, well, John Cusack. Don't get me wrong, I love the heck out of a socially awkward, passionate about music, good looking & articulate guy as much as everyone, but I was interested in seeing him portray a different sort of character. He does so well, the average guy next door with twisted desires and dark secrets, pushed to the edge of discovery by Nicholas Cage, playing - well, not Nicholas Cage. You know, the somewhat over the top slightly kooky guy? Not this time. It's a nice role reversal on the good guy/bad guy dynamic that they had in Con Air (which I'll need to watch again soon) with both parts played quite convincingly and realistically. The theme and the setting of the movie is dark, a pallor cast over it from early on that makes you want to see if your monitor or TV has changed settings while you weren't paying attention. The action mainly comes in the form of suspense, with some difficult scenes and little humor to break the mood, or even worse, mock the truth behind the tale. Given that this is based on a true story, I felt it was written, scripted, and acted out with respect for what happened, and for the victims and their families. Demons do live among us, sometimes frighteningly close, with angels as passionate about protecting people as the demons are about hurting them.
A simple game, not really a lot too it. But cute art, quick play, and not a lot of thought needed so it's a keeper.

Excellent addition to the series. If you're interested in a modern day setting with wizards, magic, and monsters, I strongly suggest that you pick up book #1, Storm Front (The Dresden Files, Book 1). You will quickly end up back to this book, and the characters will be very well developed.

Ordered this as a gift for my mom's birthday and selected overnight shipping. It arrived a day earlier than expected, nicely boxed in a sturdy presentation case with a soft cloth tucked inside. Opening it felt like unboxing something far more expensive. The ceramic band has a subtle rosy hue that's hard to capture online but looks gorgeous in person, way softer and more elegant than plain white. The crystal accents around the rim give it a refined sparkle without being over the top. Face is a comfortable mid-size, not too big or dainty. My mom hasn't taken it off since she opened it. Totally exceeded what I was expecting for the price point.
Been using this filter on my DSLR for over two years now. Still scratch-free and the glass hasn't degraded at all.
Great quality for the money. That neck zipper is super handy.

Picked this up after watching the Will Smith film and was totally surprised that it's actually short stories instead of one continuous narrative. Honestly, that format works beautifully here. Each tale builds on the Three Laws in clever ways. Asimov's logic puzzles are genuinely brilliant. A must-read for any sci-fi fan.

I want all of the guinea pig world to know these EZ whelp pads are awesome. This is so much better than fleece and totally cost effective. I have a large rabbit cage which is 17x40 and my piggies sleep in this at night and spend all day in a kiddie play pool with a fence around it...3 ft round. I was using carefresh bedding and a litter box which my boars use about half the time for pee and some poo. The carefresh is odor absorbing but definitely dusty....then along comes whelping pads. WOW!!! I BOUGHT A 36 inch pad and put that in the bottom of the kiddie pool. Its soft and wonderful for piggie feet and I only need to take a little plastic sand shocel and scoop up any poops and place in the litter box. Pee is immediately absorbed and after a week still no smell. I washed the pad in the washing machine on cold and dried on low heat, cones out like new. So I then got the slightly thicker paw print pads as poctured and one pad fits in the travel cage I have and 2 pads fit in their night home cage. I just use my toy shovel each morning and its great. I was able to get carefresh bedding on sale so I use that only in the litter box. Persobally these make the cages look neat and fresh and I think its better to not have as much paper bedding. Its cheaper than fleece and I think more absorbing. Thanks to EZ whelp...piggies have a great new source for their home needs. Pics are of bog cage, travel cage, pig pool...and one of my boars.

Been using this for about three months now to keep my labrador out of the kitchen trash. Dropped it once on tile and it didn't skip a beat. Still going strong on the original battery. She used to raid the garbage every single night, hasn't tried once since the second day. Build quality is honestly way better than I expected for the price.
Super cute, super soft and super cuddly. My daughter loved it as soon as she saw it. It is her favorite.
Awesome deal for the price. I light my grill with these all the time. It only takes me a few shavings to get the rest of the fire bundled going. For what I use it for it will last me a long time
Great size for my cockatiel. Much better than him trying to bathe in his water dish.
I love this work out, it's short and it works, I felt a difference the first week I used it.

Got the complete set on sale and honestly it's worth every penny. The production quality is fantastic - costumes, sets, the whole period vibe feels authentic. Each episode captures the books perfectly. My whole family got hooked. Can't beat this much quality entertainment for the price.

This is an awesome set. Precisely cut decals with evenly distributed adhesive. Peels well (save for small items requiring nifty fingers). Once activated it glows strongly for about two hours and faintly for another four hours. I cannot speak in long term as it was just received and installed. Will update on progress. Shipped straight from Germany and got here in exactly one week.

Awesome boot. Totally rugged and 100%water proof. I was up to my ankles in puddles of slush and water and ice/snow. I kept expecting to feel cold water rush in but it never happened and my feet stayed pretty warm. Amazing.

Honestly picked this up on a whim thinking it'd be one of those forgettable wartime musicals studios cranked out by the dozen. Boy was I wrong. The Technicolor presentation here is way sharper than I expected, colors just leap off the screen, especially during Miranda's numbers. She absolutely steals every scene she's in and the comedic timing caught me off guard. I figured the romance subplot would feel phoned in, but it actually flows pretty naturally with the musical sequences. Sure, the leading man isn't going to win any acting awards and his vocals are decent at best, but the production numbers more than make up for it. Way better than some of the bigger budget Fox musicals from the same era. Glad I took the chance on this one.

I bought these after my plastic utensils started melting and warping. I was skeptical about the material but these are great. They don't scratch my nonstick pans and they actually hold up to high heat. I use them for everything from stirring pasta sauce to flipping eggs. The best part is that I just toss them in the dishwasher when I'm done. No special treatment or oiling required like my old bamboo spoons needed. They still look brand new after months of daily use and dozens of wash cycles. I'd definitely recommend these to anyone who hates hand washing dishes but wants to protect their cookware. Also great for anyone transitioning away from plastic kitchen tools. Grabbing another set for my mom.
These pants are very comfortable to be made out of such durable material.

I was very impressed at how well made and useful this little credit card tool is. Can see many uses for it already. Quick shipping too. Free Survival Guide was a really GREAT bonus too! Will buy for birthday gifts - would fit perfect right inside a birthday card as seller suggested in a follow-up email. Emergency blankets will go in car.
The double-wall insulation on this thing is no joke. Left ice water in my car during a 90-degree day and came back to solid ice cubes still floating six hours later. Worth every penny for that alone.
Hard to tell how long it will hold up, but at this price it was still a bargain. Would purchase again.
Everything described about the item was exact,work great,Great price,this thing is awesome, planing too buy another one soon

Bought these for my detached garage and front porch. Setup took maybe ten minutes per switch, and the directions were clear enough that I didn't have to guess at anything. Now the lights come on right at dusk and shut off when I want them to. No more wasting electricity because somebody forgot to flip the switch. They've been running through rain and freezing temps with zero issues. Honestly wish I'd done this years ago. Solid product.
